member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Collections.BoundedConcurrentDictionary`2
member.summary: A thread-safe dictionary with a hard capacity ceiling. When an insert would push the count past , the entire dictionary is cleared first and then the new entry is added ("clear-all on overflow" eviction). This matches the precedent established by Brainarr's MetricsCollector and LimiterRegistry as well as Qobuzarr's SharedSystemHttpClient — clear-all is simpler than LRU and the stale state that is lost reconstructs naturally on the next operation.
member.remarks: This class is NOT ; it owns no timers or other unmanaged resources. Thread-safety guarantees mirror those of .
member.typeparam: The type of keys in the dictionary.
The type of values in the dictionary.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Collections.BoundedConcurrentDictionary`2.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{`0},System.Action{System.Int32})
member.summary: Initialises a new instance with the given capacity.
member.param: Maximum number of entries. Must be greater than zero.
Optional key comparer. Defaults to the default comparer for .
Optional callback invoked when an eviction occurs. Receives the number of entries that were cleared. Useful for metrics/logging without coupling the type to a logger.
member.exception: Thrown when is zero or negative.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Collections.BoundedConcurrentDictionary`2.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the configured capacity ceiling.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Collections.BoundedConcurrentDictionary`2.Count
member.summary: Gets the current number of entries. Snapshot — may change immediately.

member.summary: Returns a snapshot of all current keys.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Collections.BoundedConcurrentDictionary`2.Values
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of all current values. Mirrors .

member.summary: Returns true if the dictionary contains the specified key. Mirrors ; useful for "miss" caches whose values are byte sentinels and where TryGetValue is overkill.

Indexer that mirrors 's.
Get: when absent.

member.summary: Enumerates key/value pairs. Mirrors 's snapshot-style enumerator — safe under concurrent mutation but does not observe writes that happen after enumeration begins.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Diagnostics.HealthCheckHelper
member.summary: Shared scaffold for provider authentication health checks. Wraps the Stopwatch+try/catch pattern that is repeated verbatim across Tidalarr, Qobuzarr, and AppleMusicarr CheckAuthAsync implementations.
member.remarks: Each plugin's local *HealthDiagnostics class retains its provider-specific constants (provider name, authMethod, diagnosticType, capability, error messages) and calls this helper instead of hand-rolling the timing scaffold.

member.summary: Executes an authentication probe and wraps the result in a .
member.param: Async function that performs the actual authentication check and returns when the provider is authenticated and ready. The is forwarded from .
Optional diagnostic type tag passed through to . Defaults to .
Optional capability tag passed through to . Defaults to .
Message used when returns . Defaults to "Authentication failed".
Error code used when returns . Defaults to .
Error code used when throws an unexpected exception. Defaults to .

Optional delegate to format the exception message that appears in when the probe throws. When , is used directly. Use this to prepend provider-specific actionable hints (e.g. "Check your Developer Token, Team ID, and Key ID…").
Cancellation token forwarded to the probe.
member.returns: A that is healthy when the probe returns , unhealthy with when the probe returns , and unhealthy with the exception message (optionally transformed) when the probe throws.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Diagnostics.WarnOnce
member.summary: Process-scoped, per-key warn-once gate. Tracks which keys have already emitted a warn-level log entry and routes subsequent calls to a debug-level action (or a no-op if none supplied).

remarks.para: Both call-site patterns are handled by a single instance type:

item.description: Process-global single key (Qobuzarr wire-up pattern): use a private static readonly WarnOnce _warn = new(); field and call with a fixed key such as "wireup".
Per-dimension key (Brainarr tokenizer-fallback pattern): use the same instance and pass a composite key such as $"{reason}:{normalizedModelKey}".

remarks.para: The class is thread-safe: can be called concurrently from multiple threads; exactly one caller wins the TryAdd race and invokes the warn action. All other concurrent callers invoke the debug action (or do nothing).
is provided for unit-test isolation. Do not call it in production code.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Diagnostics.WarnOnce.#ctor
member.summary: Initialises a new using ordinal (case-sensitive) key comparison.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Diagnostics.WarnOnce.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{System.String})
member.summary: Initialises a new with a custom key comparer.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Diagnostics.WarnOnce.TryWarn(System.String,System.Action,System.Action)
member.summary: Invokes the first time this method is called with ; invokes on all subsequent calls with the same key.
member.param: Stable identifier for this warning site. Must not be or empty.
Action to run on the first occurrence (typically logger.Warn(...)).
Optional action to run on subsequent occurrences (typically logger.Debug(...)). If , subsequent calls are silent.
member.returns: if was invoked (first call for this key); if was invoked or the call was silently suppressed.
member.exception: Thrown when or is .
Thrown when is empty or whitespace.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Diagnostics.WarnOnce.TryWarn(System.String,System.Exception,System.Action{System.Exception},System.Action{System.Exception})
member.summary: Exception-carrying overload of . Passes the exception to both actions so callers can log it at the appropriate level.
member.param: Stable identifier for this warning site.
Exception to forward to the action.
Action receiving the exception on the first occurrence.
Optional action receiving the exception on subsequent occurrences.
member.returns: if was invoked; otherwise.

member.summary: Clears all previously seen keys, resetting the instance to its initial state.
member.remarks: Intended for unit-test isolation only. Calling this in production code will cause warn messages to re-fire.

member.summary: Thrown when authentication or authorization fails. Corresponds to HTTP 401/403 or invalid API key errors.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors.DownloadIntegrityException
member.summary:
Thrown when a downloaded file's actual byte count does not match the expected total declared by the server (via Content-Length or Content-Range).
When this exception is raised, the partial/final file has already been deleted so the caller can retry the download from scratch without stale data.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors.DownloadIntegrityException.ActualBytes
member.summary: Number of bytes actually written to disk.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors.DownloadIntegrityException.ExpectedBytes
member.summary: Number of bytes the server advertised (Content-Length or Content-Range total).

member.summary: Initialises a new .

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors.LlmErrorMapper.MapHttpError(System.String,System.Int32,System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Exception)
member.summary: Maps an HTTP status code to the appropriate , preserving an explicit hint extracted from the response headers.
member.param: The provider that returned this error.
Optional response body for parsing retry-after or error details.
Explicit retry-after hint, typically from the Retry-After response header. When non-null, this overrides any retry-after parsed from .
member.returns: An appropriate subclass.
member.remarks: Source: brainarr Phase 4a feedback ("LlmProviderException.RetryAfter is set on RateLimitException but never plumbed through adapters"). Adapters that have access to should prefer , which extracts the Retry-After header automatically.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors.LlmErrorMapper.MapHttpError(System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpResponseMessage,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Maps an to the appropriate , extracting the Retry-After header automatically and preserving it on the resulting exception (notably ).
member.param: The provider that returned this error.
Origin response. Headers are inspected for Retry-After.
Optional preloaded response body. Pass when the caller has already buffered the body to avoid re-reading the content stream (which may have already been consumed).
member.returns: An appropriate subclass.
member.remarks: Source: brainarr Phase 4a feedback. This overload is the preferred adapter-path API: it ensures the Retry-After response header always flows through to .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Errors.PagedResponseIntegrityException
member.summary: Thrown when the total number of items collected across all pages of a paged API response does not match the totalCount declared by the server.
member.remarks: Silent truncation — receiving fewer items than declared — is the most common form of this defect, but over-fetching (e.g., duplicate pages) is equally detected. Callers should surface this as a data-integrity warning rather than retrying blindly, because the mismatch often indicates a server-side bug or a pagination logic error in the plugin.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.AlbumReleaseInfoBuilder
member.summary:
Builds the three ReleaseInfo string fields — Guid, DownloadUrl, and Title — that every RicherTunes streaming-service plugin constructs from the same album + quality + artist data.
Why strings instead of a ReleaseInfo instance? Common cannot reference NzbDrone.Core.Indexers.ReleaseInfo (host assembly). Returning the three strings lets each plugin map to its own ReleaseInfo / IndexerRelease type cleanly.
Title format (default):
{Artist} - {Album} ({Year}) [{FormatMarker}] [{ExtraMarker}] [{ReleaseGroup}] // all fields {Artist} - {Album} ({Year}) [{FormatMarker}] [{ReleaseGroup}] // with year and format marker, no extra {Artist} - {Album} ({Year}) [{ReleaseGroup}] // with year, no format marker {Artist} - {Album} [{FormatMarker}] [{ReleaseGroup}] // no year, with format marker {Artist} - {Album} [{ReleaseGroup}] // no year, no format marker
GUID grammar (aligns with ):
{scheme}:album:{albumId} // no quality hint {scheme}:album:{albumId}:{qualityHint} // with quality hint
DownloadUrl format:
{scheme}://album/{albumId} // no quality hint {scheme}://album/{albumId}?quality={qualityHint} // with quality hint
This is Wave A item 8 from the May 2026 bridge-unification plan — the Guid/DownloadUrl/Title boilerplate was duplicated ~40 LOC per plugin.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadOrchestrator
member.summary:
Centralises the fire-and-forget download enqueue pattern shared by every RicherTunes streaming-service plugin's Download(...) method.
Shape replaced in each plugin (Wave A item 2):
Snapshot settings before Task.Run — prevents TOCTOU when settings change mid-download (the "ProbeOnly read twice" race documented in PR #130 finding #9).
Generate a GUID downloadId (Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N")).
Construct a tracked download item via caller-supplied item factory.
Insert into the tracker before Task.Run, so GetItems() polling never misses a just-started download.
Fire-and-forget Task.Run the actual work. The work lambda receives the SNAPSHOT, not the live settings object — defeating the race.
Return downloadId.
Snapshot strategy: option (c) — caller supplies a snapshotter lambda. This is zero-magic and explicit about which fields are included in the snapshot. Per-plugin call sites pass s => new TSettings { Field1 = s.Field1, … } or call a Clone() method if one exists. This is especially important for settings that hold reference types (lists, dicts): the snapshotter is responsible for deep-copying those fields.
Lifted from Tidalarr and AppleMusicarr as Wave A item 2 of the May 2026 bridge-unification plan.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadOrchestrator.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Create an orchestrator instance. is optional — pass to silence orchestrator-level log output (useful in tests or when the plugin's own logger already covers the surrounding context).
Snapshot settings, create a tracked item, enqueue fire-and-forget work, return downloadId.
member.typeparam: Per-plugin settings type. The caller provides the so this method is agnostic to whether TSettings has ICloneable, a copy-constructor, or deep-clone logic for reference-typed fields.
member.param: Live settings object to snapshot before Task.Run.
Process-wide tracker store. The item is inserted BEFORE Task.Run so GetItems() polling never races against a just-started download.
Pure function that produces an isolated copy of . Must copy all fields the lambda reads; reference-typed fields (lists, dicts) must be deep-copied.
Creates the tracker item. Receives the SNAPSHOT and the generated downloadId. Called synchronously before Task.Run.
The actual download logic. Receives the SNAPSHOT, downloadId, the created item (for progress updates), and the cancellation token. Executed fire-and-forget on Task.Run.
member.returns: The generated downloadId (32 hex characters, no hyphens).

Status enum for . Matches the conceptual states every Lidarr download client passes through: Queued → Downloading → Completed/Failed.
This enum lives in Common so the per-plugin tracker doesn't need to import NzbDrone.Core.Download.DownloadItemStatus in code paths that only need the internal state machine. Plugins map this to the host enum at the boundary (in their DownloadClientBase.GetItems() override).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadItem
member.summary:
Thread-safe per-download tracker DTO. Apple's AppleMusicDownloadTrackerItem and tidal's TidalDownloadItem were byte-for-byte the same pattern (Volatile.Read/Write on int status + Interlocked.Exchange on a long-bit-packed double progress). Lifted to Common as Wave A item 1 from memory/project_apple_bridge_unification_plan.md.
Plugins subclass this only if they need extra fields beyond what's here (apple/tidal currently don't — they can use this type directly).

member.summary: Time the download reached a terminal state (Completed / Failed). Backed by an Interlocked-protected ticks field — safe under concurrent observation from the retention sweep.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadTrackerStore`1
member.summary:
Process-wide tracker store for (or subclass).
The store is intentionally instance-scoped (not static) so each plugin holds ONE store across all client re-instantiations. Plugins wire it up as a static readonly field on their DownloadClientBase subclass — Lidarr can re-construct the client between queue polls, but the store survives.
Retention: completed/failed items are evicted from after the configured retention window. In-progress items are NEVER evicted (no upper bound on download duration — a stuck job stays in the queue until the operator removes it).
Lifted from apple's AppleMusicLidarrDownloadClient.ActiveDownloads + retention sweep (Wave A item 1 of the May 2026 unification plan). Tidalarr's equivalent (TidalLidarrDownloadClient.ActiveDownloads) can adopt the same store once it's available — the security fix from PR #130 review #2 finding #11 (path-traversal containment) becomes universal as a side effect.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadTrackerStore`1.#ctor(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Default retention: 30 minutes. Long enough that the Lidarr UI shows the result after a download completes; short enough that old failures don't accumulate.

member.summary: Add or replace a tracker item (typically called from Download()). Silently overwrites an existing item with the same — callers that want collision detection should use .

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadTrackerStore`1.GetSnapshot
member.summary: Snapshot of currently-tracked items. Side-effect: evicts completed/failed items past the retention window (FIFO sweep). Plugins call this from DownloadClientBase.GetItems().

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeDownloadTrackerStore`1.Remove(System.String,System.Boolean,`0@,System.Action{System.Exception})
member.summary:
Remove a tracker entry. With set, also delete the item's directory.
If the directory delete fails (UnauthorizedAccessException, file locked by AV/indexer, PathTooLongException, …) the exception is reported via the optional callback so the caller can log it through its NLog instance. Without the callback, errors are swallowed silently — matches the pre-v1.9.0 behavior for callers that haven't migrated yet.
Returns true if the item was found and removed from the dictionary, regardless of whether the directory delete succeeded.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2
member.summary:
Generic singleton-runtime cache for Lidarr-native bridge plugins. Solves the "host instantiates indexer/dc directly via reflection, but we need a process-wide runtime keyed on auth-critical settings" pattern that every plugin has independently re-derived (apple's AppleMusicLidarrRuntimeProvider, tidal's EnsureServicesInitialized, etc.).
What the cache provides:
Gated initialization — a ensures concurrent GetAsync callers see one runtime, not N parallel constructions.
Auth-key invalidation — subclass returns an opaque key string from auth settings; when the key changes, a new runtime is built.
Deferred-disposal graveyard — previous runtime is parked for seconds before its DisposeAsync runs, so in-flight callers holding the prior runtime via captured locals don't crash with ObjectDisposedException (apple PR #130 review #1 finding #4).
Bounded graveyard — under credential-thrash, the oldest entries are force-disposed to keep the queue bounded (apple PR #130 review #2 finding #4).
Fire-and-forget disposal — DisposeAsync runs on a background so request threads don't block on HttpClient shutdown.
Test-only reset — drains both the cache and the graveyard.
Subclass contract:
public sealed class MyRuntimeCache : HostBridgeRuntimeCache<MyRuntime, MySettings> { protected override string ComputeAuthKey(MySettings settings) => SHA256(settings.ApiKey + "|" + settings.UserToken);
protected override Task<MyRuntime?> CreateAsync(MySettings settings, CancellationToken ct) => MyRuntime.CreateAsync(settings.ApiKey, settings.UserToken, ct); }
Lifted as Wave D item 6 from memory/project_apple_bridge_unification_plan.md.
member.typeparam: The plugin's runtime/service-collection root type. Must implement so the graveyard can dispose it cleanly.
The plugin's host-shape settings (whichever type carries the auth-critical fields).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2.GraveyardLingerSeconds
member.summary: Linger window before a retired runtime is disposed. Default 60s.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2.GraveyardMaxSize
member.summary: Hard cap on graveyard size. Force-dispose oldest entry on overflow.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2.ComputeAuthKey(`1)
member.summary: Compute the auth-fingerprint key. Used for cache-hit comparison. Should hash the auth-critical fields ONLY (changing a non-critical field shouldn't invalidate the cached runtime). Return value is treated as opaque — typically SHA256-hex.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2.CreateAsync(`1,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Build a fresh runtime from settings. Return null to signal "settings incomplete / missing credentials" — the cache will propagate the null up to the caller without storing it.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2.GetAsync(`1,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Return a runtime for the given settings — cached if auth fields haven't changed, freshly constructed (with the prior runtime parked for deferred disposal) otherwise. Returns null if returns null.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.HostBridgeRuntimeCache`2.ResetAsync
member.summary: Test-only reset: dispose the current runtime AND drain the graveyard. Drops both the cache slot and any parked runtimes so the next test starts clean.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PathTraversalGuard
member.summary:
Defense-in-depth helpers for keeping a plugin's download output under its configured root.
Threat model and limitations: the guard performs LEXICAL canonicalization via Path.GetFullPath, which resolves ./../separator quirks but does NOT resolve symlinks (Linux), junctions (Windows), or NTFS reparse points. If an attacker can plant a symlink inside the configured download root pointing to /etc, the guard accepts paths under that symlink even though the resolved physical target escapes the root. This is acceptable when the threat model is "hostile metadata source can inject only string segments" (artist/album names from MusicBrainz / search results / manual artist creation in Lidarr's UI) — those threats can't write filesystem symlinks. If your threat model includes a writable-inside-root attacker, add an explicit symlink resolution step at the call site (e.g. new DirectoryInfo(path).ResolveLinkTarget(returnFinalTarget: true) on .NET 6+).
Lidarr-native bridge plugins (apple/tidalarr/qobuzarr) build per-download paths by Path.Combine(downloadRoot, artistName, albumTitle). The artist/album segments come from a metadata source (MusicBrainz, search results) that's normally trusted — but Lidarr also allows manual artist creation, and a hostile search-result feed could inject a segment like "../../" that resolves OUT of the download root once Directory.CreateDirectory + Directory.Delete(recursive: true) walk the path.
Both (per-segment scrubbing) AND (canonical-form whole-path containment check) need to hold at the call site. Either alone is incomplete: Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars() doesn't include . so ".." survives sanitization; conversely root checks are only useful when the segments are already strict.
Originally implemented in AppleMusicLidarrDownloadClient (PR #130 adversarial review finding #11). Lifted here so tidalarr and qobuzarr can adopt the same guard without re-implementing — the same exposure exists in both.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PathTraversalGuard.SanitizeSegment(System.String)
member.summary:
Sanitize a single path segment by replacing invalid filename characters with underscores, then collapsing pure-dot segments (., .., ...) which would otherwise resolve as up-tree references during Path.GetFullPath.
Returns "_" for null / empty / whitespace input (never returns a value that Path.Combine would interpret as a no-op or up-reference).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PathTraversalGuard.OsAwareComparison
member.summary: Path comparison is filesystem-case-sensitive: Linux is case-sensitive, Windows / macOS (default HFS+/APFS) are case-insensitive. Using OrdinalIgnoreCase universally would let an attacker on Linux escape via a case-twin: /mnt/Music root would accept output paths under /mnt/music as "within root" even though those are two distinct directories. This `OsAwareComparison` is the safety boundary (PR #130 review #2 finding #3).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PathTraversalGuard.IsPathWithinRoot(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Verify the canonical form of resolves to within (or, optionally, — used for fallback dirs like %TEMP%/<plugin> in probe-only modes that allow blank-root configurations).
Comparison case-sensitivity matches the underlying filesystem (case-sensitive on Linux, case-insensitive on Windows/macOS) to prevent the case-twin escape vector. Returns true if the canonical output equals or is a descendant of the root.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PathTraversalGuard.ContainsTraversalAttempt(System.String)
member.summary:
Predicate-only probe: returns true if contains a path-traversal attempt (literal .. segments, URL-encoded variants). Use for input validation where you want to REJECT (or audit-log) traversal attempts without normalizing or building a real path — e.g. URL validators, text-field validators, log-redaction triggers.
This is the predicate sibling of . Use when you want to neutralize the traversal and keep going. Use ContainsTraversalAttempt when you want to REJECT the input entirely (return a 400, surface an error, log a suspicious-input alert).
Detection covers:
Literal .. followed by / or \ (most common case)
/.. or \.. trailing or before another separator
Exact .. with no surrounding chars
URL-encoded variants %2e%2e (any case combination) followed by /, \, %2f, or %5c
NOT flagged: dots inside a name (foo..bar, artist.album.2024) — the predicate requires a separator-adjacency to count as traversal. This matches the actual escape vector (separator-bounded .. segments resolve via Path.GetFullPath) without false-positiving common filename patterns.
Returns false for null / empty / whitespace input.
Wave-26 #45: lifted from a submodule-local patch (existed at apple's pinned SHA 639d573 but never made it back to canonical Common; brainarr's SecureUrlValidator + SubscriptionCredentialLoader call this method).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PlaceholderSearchUri
member.summary:
Build + parse {scheme}://search?query={encoded} placeholder URIs.
Lidarr-native bridge plugins (apple, tidal) use this trick to get a search query through Lidarr's HttpIndexerBase pipeline. The base class expects an HTTP request to be issued in GetRequestGenerator; the actual search has to happen asynchronously in FetchReleases. The compromise is a placeholder HttpRequest whose URL encodes the query, and which the FetchReleases override unwraps when iterating the request chain.
Identical algorithm in apple's AppleMusicLidarrIndexer.TryExtractQuery and tidal's TidalLidarrIndexer.TryExtractQuery — lifted as Wave A item 5 from memory/project_apple_bridge_unification_plan.md.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PlaceholderSearchUri.Build(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Build {scheme}://search?query={URL-encoded query}. Caller is responsible for validating the query first; the builder is defensive (empty query produces a shape-valid URI with empty query parameter — easier than throwing).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PlaceholderSearchUri.TryExtractQuery(System.String,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Extract the query parameter from a placeholder URI. Returns false (with set to empty) for any URL that doesn't match {scheme}://search?query=... with a non-empty query value.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PrefixedReleaseGuidParser
member.summary:
Parse album IDs from Lidarr ReleaseInfo.Guid / ReleaseInfo.InfoUrl shapes shared by all RicherTunes streaming-service plugins.
The supported GUID grammar is:
[{indexerId}_]{scheme}:album:{id}[:{extra}]
Where:
{indexerId}_ — optional. Lidarr prefixes search-result GUIDs with the indexer's numeric ID followed by an underscore (e.g. 2_applemusic:album:111). The parser strips it transparently.
{scheme} — case-insensitive. Each plugin owns a literal (applemusic, tidal, etc.).
:album: — fixed segment. Track-level GUIDs are out of scope for now; callers expecting album extraction get null back when the type segment isn't album (case-insensitive).
{id} — required, non-empty. Apple uses numeric, tidal uses numeric, qobuz uses arbitrary tokens. The parser doesn't validate format.
{extra} — optional fourth segment plugins can use for service-specific metadata (e.g. tidal encodes quality here: tidal:album:99999:Lossless). Ignored by this parser.
The fallback recognizes any URL whose path contains a /album/{id}/ segment.
Why string params (not ReleaseInfo): Common doesn't reference NzbDrone.Core. Plugins call this with release.Guid and release.InfoUrl extracted at the call site, which lets the helper stay in Common's clean dependency tree (only BCL).
May 2026 unification — this is Wave A item 3 from memory/project_apple_bridge_unification_plan.md. Apple and Tidal each had ~60 LOC of identical extraction logic with only the scheme literal differing.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PrefixedReleaseGuidParser.ExtractAlbumId(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Convenience: GUID first, fall back to InfoUrl. Returns empty string (not null) so callers can throw if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(id)) without null handling.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PrefixedReleaseGuidParser.ExtractAlbumIdFromGuid(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Parse the GUID grammar described in . Returns null for any shape that doesn't match (caller should try as a fallback).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.HostBridge.PrefixedReleaseGuidParser.ExtractAlbumIdFromUrlPath(System.String)
member.summary: Find /album/{id}/ in a URL's path. Returns null when the URL is empty, not parseable, or has no album segment.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Hosting.PluginConfigRoots
member.summary: Resolves the per-plugin configuration root directory. The chain matches the convention used across Lidarr.* plugins: 1. LIDARR_PLUGIN_CONFIG environment variable (override) 2. /config when present (Docker hotio/linuxserver convention) 3. Windows: %AppData% 4. Linux/macOS: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME, then $HOME/.config 5. Last resort: /config/<appName>

remarks.para: This consolidates the hand-rolled chain that several plugins (tidalarr, applemusicarr, qobuzarr) replicated independently, fixing two subtle bugs commonly seen in those copies:

item.description: HOME may point at /root for non-root container users; /config is preferred when present.
XDG_CONFIG_HOME is honored (POSIX convention) before falling back to $HOME/.config.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Hosting.PluginLifecycle
member.summary:
Static registry for plugin teardown hooks. Plugin module Dispose() methods call once; each registered action is invoked in reverse-registration order (LIFO) so inner resources unwind before outer ones.
Why static? The consumers (e.g. MetricsCollector, SharedSystemHttpClient) are themselves static classes. Wrapping them in an instance interface would require changes throughout every consumer. The static registry is the simplest forwarder and matches the existing pattern in .
member.remarks: Thread-safe. All mutations are guarded by _lock. Shutdown is idempotent: subsequent calls after the first are no-ops.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Hosting.PluginLifecycle.AttachLogger(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Attaches an optional logger. Failures in individual shutdown hooks will be logged at Warning level rather than silently swallowed.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Hosting.PluginLifecycle.RegisterShutdown(System.String,System.Action)
member.summary: Registers a shutdown action under the given name. Actions are invoked in LIFO order by . Registrations after has been called are silently ignored (the plugin is already being torn down).
member.param: Human-readable name used in log messages on failure.
Action to invoke on plugin teardown.
member.exception: If or is null.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Hosting.PluginLifecycle.Shutdown
member.summary: Invokes all registered shutdown actions in reverse-registration order (LIFO). Exceptions thrown by individual hooks are caught and logged; they do not prevent subsequent hooks from running. After the first call, subsequent calls are no-ops.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Hosting.PluginLifecycle.ResetForTesting
member.summary: Resets the registry to its initial state. Intended for use in unit tests only.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.IMetricsRecorder
member.summary: Dimensional metrics interface for plugins. The pre-existing static counter is intentionally untouched (legacy callers continue to compile); this interface provides the name+value+tags shape that brainarr-style providers need for per-provider/per-model labels.

remarks.para: Source: brainarr Phase 4e adoption feedback ("common's Metrics is static no-op counters only; brainarr needs name+tags shape for per-provider/per-model dimensional metrics").

Common ships as the default no-op implementation and as a fanout adapter for plugins that want to forward events to Prometheus, StatsD, OpenTelemetry, etc. Common itself does not take a dependency on any metrics library.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.IMetricsRecorder.Increment(System.String,System.Double,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Records a counter increment.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.IMetricsRecorder.Gauge(System.String,System.Double,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Records a gauge value.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.IMetricsRecorder.Histogram(System.String,System.Double,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Records a histogram sample.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.NullMetricsRecorder
member.summary: No-op default . Use when no metrics backend is configured. All methods are safe to call concurrently and never allocate.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.NullMetricsRecorder.Instance
member.summary: The singleton no-op instance.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.ObservableMetricsRecorder
member.summary: that fans out every recorded sample to subscribed observers as a . Plugins that want to integrate with Prometheus/StatsD/etc. subscribe and translate events on their side, keeping common free of metrics-library dependencies.
member.remarks: Subscribers are invoked synchronously inside the recording call. Exceptions raised by subscribers are swallowed (best-effort fanout) — observability code must not break the path of the operation it instruments.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds
member.summary: Standard event IDs for LLM provider operations. Use these with ILogger to enable consistent log filtering and alerting. Event IDs are grouped by category: - 2000-2009: Authentication events - 2010-2019: Request lifecycle events - 2020-2029: Rate limiting events - 2030-2039: Health check events

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.AUTH_SUCCESS
member.summary: Authentication succeeded (API key valid, OAuth token accepted).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.AUTH_FAIL
member.summary: Authentication failed (invalid key, expired token, unauthorized).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.REQUEST_START
member.summary: LLM request started (prompt sent to provider).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.REQUEST_COMPLETE
member.summary: LLM request completed successfully.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.REQUEST_ERROR
member.summary: LLM request failed with error.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.RATE_LIMITED
member.summary: Rate limit hit (HTTP 429 or equivalent).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.RATE_LIMIT_RECOVERED
member.summary: Recovered from rate limit (successful request after backoff).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.HEALTH_CHECK_PASS
member.summary: Provider health check passed.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LlmEventIds.HEALTH_CHECK_FAIL
member.summary: Provider health check failed.

Event id attached to the emitted log entry.
member.returns: if the warning was emitted; if suppressed.

member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\bsk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\\b
Options:
RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ Match the string "sk-".
○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Za-z] greedily at least 20 times.
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.remarks:

Options:
RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ Loop exactly 3 times.
○ Match a character in the set [0-9] atomically at least 1 and at most 3 times.
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-9] greedily at least 1 and at most 3 times.
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LogRedactor.CreditCardPattern
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\b(?:\\d[ -]*?){13,16}\\b
Options:
RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at a word boundary.
○ Loop greedily at least 13 and at most 16 times.
○ Match a Unicode digit.
○ Match a character in the set [ -] lazily any number of times.
○ Match if at a word boundary.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.LogRedactor.RedactException(System.Exception)
member.summary: Returns a redacted single-line representation of an exception suitable for logging in token-handling code paths. Walks the inner-exception chain and applies to each Message. The full StackTrace is intentionally omitted because it can contain bound parameter values (URLs with embedded auth, etc.) that vary by framework and are not covered by structured redaction patterns.
member.param: The exception to render. Null returns empty string.
member.returns: A redacted, exception-type-prefixed message chain.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext
member.summary:
Lightweight async-local scope for structured plugin log lines.
Pushes a named context onto an stack so that caller code can format consistent log prefixes without taking a hard dependency on any particular logging framework.
Usage:
using var ctx = PluginLogContext.Push("Tidalarr", "Search", provider: "tidal"); logger.Info($"{ctx.LinePrefix()}fetching tracks…");
Nested pushes stack; disposing a child scope restores the parent. Multiple concurrent async paths each maintain their own scope without bleeding into one another (AsyncLocal semantics).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.Push(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Opens a new log context scope and makes it the current scope on the calling async execution context. Dispose the returned object to pop.
member.param: Optional correlation ID. When omitted a new is used.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.Current
member.summary: The innermost active on the current async execution context, or null if no scope is active.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.PluginName
member.summary: Plugin name, e.g. "Tidalarr".

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.Operation
member.summary: Operation label, e.g. "Search" or "GetRecommendations".

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.CorrelationId
member.summary: Correlation ID for this scope. Defaults to a GUID (no hyphens) when not supplied to .

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.Provider
member.summary: Optional provider identifier, e.g. "tidal" or "qobuz".

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.LinePrefix
member.summary:
Returns a structured log line prefix in the form [op:correlationId:provider] (provider segment omitted when null). Callers interpolate this into their existing logger calls:
logger.Info($"{ctx.LinePrefix()}fetched {count} tracks");

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.PluginLogContext.Dispose
member.summary: Pops this scope and restores the parent (if any).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.Scrub
member.summary:
Centralized scrubbing helpers for preventing secrets from reaching logs.
Three surfaces are covered:
— truncate an opaque token to its leading visible chars.
— redact values of known sensitive header names.
— replace values of known sensitive URL query parameters.
These helpers are intentionally thin: they do not depend on and produce strings callers can interpolate into any logger without friction.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.Scrub.Secret(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Returns a redacted representation of a secret token that preserves only the leading characters: "sk-abc...xyz" → "sk-***".
When is null, empty, or shorter than the entire value is replaced with "***".
member.param: The secret to scrub. Null is treated as empty.
Number of characters to keep. Defaults to 3. Must be non-negative.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.Scrub.Headers(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary:
Returns a copy of where the values of known sensitive header names are replaced with "***".
Comparison is case-insensitive. The set of sensitive headers includes: Authorization, X-API-Key, X-Auth-Token, X-Access-Token, Cookie, Set-Cookie, Proxy-Authorization.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.Scrub.Url(System.String)
member.summary:
Returns a copy of where the values of known sensitive query parameters are replaced with "***".
Known sensitive parameter names (case-insensitive): api_key, apikey, api-key, token, access_token, refresh_token, key, secret, client_secret, password, pwd, authorization, bearer, auth.
The URL scheme, host, path, and non-sensitive parameters are preserved.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Observability.Scrub.UrlAndStripQuery(System.String)
member.summary:
Defensive full-strip variant of : drops the entire query string regardless of parameter name. Use when the URL may contain caller-controlled tokens the selective regex doesn't recognize (e.g. third-party APIs that use non-canonical token parameter names). Returns the scheme + host + path only.
Returns for null/empty input; returns the original string unchanged when new Uri(url) throws — log redaction must not crash a log statement.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Providers.ClaudeCode.ClaudeCodeStreamParser
member.summary: Parses NDJSON streaming output from Claude Code CLI when using --output-format stream-json.

remarks.para: Claude Code CLI stream format emits newline-delimited JSON objects. Each line is a complete JSON object representing a streaming event.

Event types include: - message_start: Initial message metadata - content_block_start: Beginning of a content block - content_block_delta: Incremental content (text, thinking) - content_block_stop: End of a content block - message_delta: Final message metadata with stop_reason - message_stop: End of message - result: Final result with usage stats

member.summary: Parses CLI stream events into LLM chunks.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache
member.summary:
Short-lived in-process cache that records whether a local AI backend (e.g. Ollama / LM Studio) is known-down due to a connection-class failure.
When a call fails with a socket-level error (connection refused, host unreachable, name resolution failure) the cache records the backend as "down" for seconds. Subsequent callers asking within the grace window get an immediate fast-fail answer — without issuing any HTTP request or burning a retry budget — reducing the thunderstorm of retries visible in the logs when the user's local backend is simply not running.
Thread-safety: all mutations go through ; no additional locking is required.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.DefaultGraceSeconds
member.summary: Default grace period in seconds. A backend marked down will remain short-circuited for this many seconds before a live probe is attempted again. Long enough to absorb a UI "Test → form reload" cycle (~5 s) while being short enough that a user who actually starts their backend gets a live check within half a minute.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.Shared
member.summary: Gets the process-wide singleton instance.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.#ctor
member.summary: Creates an instance that uses the real wall clock and the default grace period.

member.summary: Creates an instance with an injected time provider for testing.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.#ctor(System.TimeProvider,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an instance with an injected System.TimeProvider and a custom grace period.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.MarkDown(System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Records that the backend identified by / failed with a connection-class exception. The backend is considered "down" for seconds from now.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.MarkUp(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Records that the backend identified by / responded successfully. Clears any existing down-state immediately.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.IsKnownDown(System.String,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Returns true if the backend is known-down and the grace window has not yet expired, in which case callers should fail fast rather than retrying. is a human-readable explanation suitable for a Debug log line.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.ClearAllForTests

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache.IsConnectionClassFailure(System.Exception)
member.summary:
Determines whether is a connection-class failure that justifies marking a backend as down.
Conservative scope — only wrapping a qualifies. This avoids false-positives for transient HTTP errors (4xx, 5xx) or timeouts that could stem from a slow-but-alive backend.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Llm.LlmPromptSanitizer
member.summary: Sanitizer for LLM prompts. Defends against prompt-injection / jailbreak attempts, control-character payload obfuscation, and unbounded resource usage. Cross-plugin core for any plugin shipping LLM-derived recommendations.
member.remarks:
Implements defense-in-depth with multiple sanitization layers:
Truncation to
Control-character + zero-width / directional-override Unicode removal
Known-pattern injection removal (operates on the lower-cased copy)
Whitespace normalization
Sensitive-data redaction (API keys, embedded credentials, emails, password=value pairs)
Final injection-attempt detection — falls back to if anything survived

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Llm.LlmPromptSanitizer.MaxPromptLength
member.summary: Maximum prompt length before truncation. Default 10,000 chars.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Llm.LlmPromptSanitizer.MaxRegexProcessingLength
member.summary: Maximum chunk size used when slicing very long inputs for regex passes.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Llm.LlmPromptSanitizer.RegexTimeoutMs
member.summary: Per-regex evaluation timeout (ms).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Llm.LlmPromptSanitizer.SafeDefaultPrompt
member.summary: Generic safe-default prompt returned when the input is unrecoverable.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize
member.summary: Context-specific encoding and sanitization helpers. Use these at the point of use (URL building, file naming, etc.).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize.UrlComponent(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a URL component using RFC 3986 rules.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize.PathSegment(System.String)
member.summary: Produces a safe file/folder name segment for the current OS. Removes invalid chars, trims, and guards Windows reserved device names.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize.FileNameSegment(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Produces a safe file/folder name segment that PRESERVES READABILITY for streaming service metadata. Use when sanitizing artist/album/track names emitted by a download client into a per-track output path.
Behavior vs the lower-level :
Path separators (/, \) and whitespace chars (\n, \r, \t) are replaced with a single space, then runs of spaces are collapsed — "AC/DC" → "AC DC", NOT "ACDC".
Zero-width characters (​, ‌, ‍, ﻿) are stripped — defeats the visual-spoof vector where a streaming service emits two visually-identical names that compare unequal at the byte level.
Empty / whitespace / null input returns (default "Unknown") so the result is always a valid path segment — callers can pass e.g. "Unknown Artist" for a more descriptive placeholder.
Pure-dot segments (.., ...) are prefixed with _ so they retain identity for debugging without resolving as a parent- directory reference.
Windows reserved device names (CON / NUL / LPT1 / etc.) are prefixed with _ for cross-platform portability (handled by underneath).
This is the security-correct successor to the obsolete FileNameSanitizer.SanitizeFileName. Callers in BaseStreamingDownloadClient + StreamingPluginMixins migrate to this; FileNameSanitizer remains alive only for external plugin callers pending their own Phase 2 cleanup.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize.IsSafePath(System.String)
member.summary:
Path traversal guard with full normalisation.
Checks that no path segment, after all normalisation passes, is a plain ".." that would traverse upward. Normalisation applied (in order): 1. Null-byte / control-character rejection. 2. Reject extended-length UNC prefix (\\?\). 3. Percent-decode once (URI %XX → char). 4. NFKC unicode normalisation (collapses fullwidth dots, etc.). 5. Replace all separator variants with the platform separator. 6. Split into segments and reject any segment that is exactly "..".
Well-formed callers are unaffected: simple relative paths, absolute OS paths, and paths where ".." appears as a substring of a filename component continue to be accepted (or rejected) exactly as before.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize.DisplayText(System.String)
member.summary: Escapes for HTML rendering. Do not use for URL building.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.Sanitize.RedactUrls(System.String)
member.summary: Redacts sensitive parts of URLs (query strings, auth tokens) for safe logging. Use this when including exception messages or URLs in logs/error messages.
member.param: Text that may contain URLs or sensitive data.
member.returns: Text with URLs redacted to scheme://host/path[?...]

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.SecureMemory
member.summary: Low-level helpers for zeroing sensitive byte buffers and PEM key strings to reduce their heap-residency window after use.

remarks.para: ZeroBytes — deterministic overwrite of a Span<byte>. When the caller owns the buffer (e.g., a heap-allocated array, a stack-allocated span, or an ArrayPool-rented segment), this ensures the sensitive bytes are cleared before the memory is returned or collected. Use this for ECDsa key material, derived key bytes, decrypted payloads, etc.
ZeroPemKey — best-effort overwrite of the char data inside a runtime-allocated PEM string, followed by nulling the caller's reference.

item.description: .NET strings are immutable by contract. ZeroPemKey uses MemoryMarshal.AsBytes to reinterpret the string's char span as bytes and overwrite them in place. This is technically outside the CLR's public API contract for strings, but works reliably on .NET 6–10 for non-interned heap strings (i.e., strings constructed at runtime from file I/O or network reads, which is where PEM data comes from).

Do NOT call on interned strings (compile-time literals, or strings that have been passed to ). Overwriting an interned string corrupts shared state and will cause unpredictable failures. PEM keys loaded at runtime from disk or the network are never interned.

The GC may have already relocated the string's backing buffer before this method is called (during a compaction GC). The overwrite always hits the current location; stale copies at old addresses will be collected normally but are not zeroed. This is a best-effort measure that significantly reduces — but does not eliminate — the heap-residency window.

For a cryptographic-strength guarantee, store key material in a byte[] pinned with GCHandle.Alloc(buf, GCHandleType.Pinned) and call when done, or use System.Security.SecureString for user-credential scenarios.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.SecureMemory.ZeroBytes(System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Overwrites every byte in with zero.
member.param: The span of bytes to zero. May be empty.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.SecureMemory.ZeroPemKey(System.String@)
member.summary: Best-effort overwrite of the char data inside a runtime PEM string, then sets the caller's reference to .
member.param: Reference to the PEM string to zero. Must not be an interned string — see class-level remarks for full caveats. Safe to call when already .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.DataProtectionTokenProtector
member.summary: IDataProtection-based protector with optional key persistence and key protection via certificate or OS-specific mechanisms.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.DataProtectionTokenProtector.GetDefaultKeysDir
member.summary: Resolves a writable directory for the DataProtection key ring, trying multiple candidates in order before falling back to Path.GetTempPath(). Designed for Lidarr Docker containers (hotio / linuxserver) where $HOME can be empty if the container PUID/PGID isn't reflected in /etc/passwd — the original implementation returned a relative .config/... path in that case which resolved against the cwd (/app/bin), a read-only mount.
member.remarks:
Candidate order:
$XDG_DATA_HOME — the standard XDG location for persistent user data; Lidarr Docker images typically set this to /config.
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME — the standard XDG location for user config.
Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData — on Linux, falls back to ~/.local/share when XDG isn't set; on Windows, AppData/Local.
$HOME/.local/share — explicit XDG fallback when SpecialFolder resolution is broken.
Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData — Windows AppData/Roaming; on Linux, ~/.config.
$HOME/.config — explicit fallback when SpecialFolder.ApplicationData returns empty.
Path.GetTempPath() — LAST RESORT (writable but ephemeral). Caller emits a warning when this hits.
Each candidate is only used if it produces a non-empty rooted (absolute) path. Empty or relative candidates are skipped — the bug this fixes was caused by silently accepting "" + "/.config/..." → a path that resolved relative to cwd.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.DataProtectionTokenProtector.GetDefaultKeysDir(System.String@)
member.summary: Single-shot overload that returns the FIRST candidate path (rooted, non-empty). Kept for back-compat — the factory's TryCreateDataProtection now iterates the full chain via and write-probes each (adversarial-review F5: previously the chain stopped at the first rooted candidate even when that candidate was unwritable).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.DataProtectionTokenProtector.EnumerateKeysDirCandidates
member.summary: Enumerates all viable key-ring directory candidates in priority order. The factory iterates and write-probes each, falling through to the next on failure (adversarial-review F5).
member.remarks:
Each entry is (path, name). The path is always rooted + non-empty (the v1.9.2 rule that prevents the original Lidarr-Docker relative-path bug). The name is a human-readable label for diagnostics.
Adversarial-review F7: on Windows, ApplicationData (Roaming) is preferred over LocalApplicationData so DPAPI ciphertext survives profile-roam in domain-joined Windows installs. On Linux/macOS, LocalApplicationData is preferred because it maps to ~/.local/share — the XDG-canonical location for persistent user data (ApplicationData maps to ~/.config, which is for config, not state).
The final entry is unconditional and uses Path.GetTempPath() so the chain always yields at least one candidate even when every other resolver returns empty.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.DataProtectionTokenProtector.TryConfigureAzureKeyVaultViaReflection(Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection.IDataProtectionBuilder,System.Uri)
member.summary:
Configures `options.ProtectKeysWithAzureKeyVault(keyId, new DefaultAzureCredential())` via reflection so the Azure.Identity / Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys assemblies are only resolved when AKV is actually opted into. Without this indirection, every consuming plugin's merged DLL has a hard AssemblyRef to Azure.Identity and ships (or fails to load if stripped) ~10 MB of Azure SDK assemblies.
Returns true when AKV configuration succeeded; false (without throwing) when the Azure assemblies aren't on disk — the caller treats that as "AKV not available" and falls back to the local DataProtection key store.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.NullTokenProtector
member.summary: Pass-through "protector" that returns plaintext bytes verbatim. Used as a graceful-degradation fallback by when the configured backend (DataProtection, DPAPI, Keychain, Secret Service) fails to initialise — typically because the key-ring directory is not writable (Lidarr Docker container with broken $HOME and the install dir mounted read-only).

remarks.para: This protector does NOT encrypt anything. It exists so the plugin remains usable when proper protection can't initialise, rather than throwing on every set_ApiKey and bricking the settings UI. The wrapping records as "null" in the protected blob, so the on-disk format is identifiable as unprotected at a glance (lpc:ps:v1:bnVsbA:... — base64-url("null") prefix).

Operators who want hard-failure instead of graceful degradation should set LP_COMMON_REQUIRE_PROTECTOR=true; the factory will then propagate the initialisation exception rather than substituting this type.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.SecretServiceTokenProtector
member.summary: Linux Secret Service-backed protector via 'secret-tool'. Falls back to exception if the helper is unavailable. Stores a random AES key under service 'LPC.TokenProtector' and account 'Default'.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.StringTokenProtector.ProtectedPrefix
member.summary: Envelope prefix for ciphertext from a real protection backend.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.StringTokenProtector.PlaintextPrefix
member.summary: Envelope prefix for blobs from — plaintext (not encrypted). The prefix is intentionally DIFFERENT from so an audit query (LIKE 'lpc:ps:v1:%') does not match unprotected entries. Adversarial-review F3 (v1.9.2): previous design embedded the algorithm id "null" inside the same lpc:ps:v1: envelope where it was visually indistinguishable from a base64-encoded algorithm-id slot of a real ciphertext — operators searching for "encrypted blobs" would have treated null-mode entries as protected.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.StringTokenProtector.NullAlgorithmId
member.summary: Algorithm id that returns. Drives the envelope-prefix decision in .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory
member.summary: Builds an from environment configuration, with graceful degradation to when the configured backend can't initialise.

remarks.para: Diagnostics surface is public so downstream plugins — which internalise Common via ILRepack — can read the flag and snapshot from their plugin-startup code and surface a "secrets are stored as plaintext" warning to the operator's log. Before v1.9.3 this class was internal, which silently broke the loud-at-startup contract — internalised types can't be reached even with reflection across plugin ALCs.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.IsDegradedToPlaintext
member.summary: Set to after the factory has fallen back to due to a backend initialisation failure. Exposed so callers (e.g. plugin-startup logging code) can confirm whether secrets are being stored encrypted vs. as plaintext on this run. Volatile read is sufficient — the field is only written once during the first call before the result is published to callers.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.LastDiagnostics
member.summary: Diagnostic snapshot describing how the active protector was constructed. Populated by the first call. Useful for plugin startup logs (operators want to see which backend won) and for regression tests that assert the fallback chain.

member.name: E:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.DegradationDetected
member.summary: Fires whenever publishes a degradation diagnostic — i.e. when the active call falls back to because no real backend could initialise. Subscribers can surface the warning beyond the static snapshot (adversarial-review F8). Adding this event is additive; plugins that already poll at startup don't need to change.
member.remarks: Subscribers MUST be exception-safe. The event invocation is inside PublishDiagnostics, on the same thread as CreateFromEnvironment; an exception from a subscriber will propagate to the caller. Static-event subscription has the usual lifetime risk: if a long-lived subscriber holds plugin-scoped state, the plugin's ALC can't unload until the subscription is removed.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.LogDegradationOnce(System.Action{System.String})
member.summary: One-shot warning logger for plugins to call from credential-handling hot paths (set_ApiKey, settings save, etc.). At-most-once per process lifetime: subsequent calls are silent no-ops. Returns if this call emitted a warning, otherwise.
member.param: Sink that emits the formatted warning line to the operator's log. Plugin code typically passes logger.Warn or equivalent.
member.remarks: Adversarial-review F8: the diagnostics surface in v1.9.2/v1.9.3 was a static snapshot — plugins had to read it at startup explicitly. This helper closes the gap so a degraded session can also be surfaced from the path the operator actually exercises (when they try to save a credential). It logs at most once because the alternative (per-call) would spam the log.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.ProbePayload
member.summary: Probe payload written by . Spells out "LPC-PROBE" in UTF-8 so a probe file left behind by a crashed process is identifiable by an operator inspecting the dir. Non-empty (8 bytes) so write-then-truncate filesystems can't pass the probe falsely (F4).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.IsExpectedBackendInitFailure(System.Exception)
member.summary:
Filters which exception types are eligible for the graceful-degradation fallback path. Adversarial-review finding F6 (v1.9.2): the previous catch (Exception) swallowed , , and (more importantly) signals from a corrupted keychain or key ring — those should propagate so the operator sees the real problem, not a misleading "we silently fell back to plaintext".
Eligible (silently degraded): - family (file not found, sharing violation, disk full, etc.) - (the original bug — read-only mount) - (e.g. secret-service on a host without libsecret) - / (native deps missing) - when the underlying message identifies a missing path/feature

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.ForbiddenSystemPaths
member.summary: Reserved system paths the factory refuses to use even if the operator supplied them via LP_COMMON_KEYS_PATH. Writing secrets into these directories — even probe files — would be a serious misconfiguration the factory should not silently honour. Adversarial-review F4 (v1.9.2): the previous EnsureKeysDirIsWritable happily probed any rooted path.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.EnsureKeysDirIsWritable(System.String)
member.summary: Tries to create + write + read-back a probe file in so we surface "directory not writable" failures here (with a clearer error) instead of inside DataProtectionProvider.Create at the first Protect call. Cleans up the probe file after.
member.remarks:
Adversarial-review F4 hardening (v1.9.3 → v1.9.4):
Refuses well-known system paths so an operator typo (LP_COMMON_KEYS_PATH=/etc) doesn't silently scribble a probe file into critical system dirs.
Writes a non-empty payload () instead of a 0-byte file. Some POSIX overlay/sshfs filesystems happily accept 0-byte writes but fail on real content, so the probe must mirror what DataProtection actually does.
Uses FileMode.CreateNew + FileShare.None so the probe can't be hijacked by a concurrent process between create and delete.
Always cleans up the probe on the way out, even if the inner write or read-back throws.
Reads the probe back and verifies the payload round-tripped — guards against write-then-truncate filesystems (some NFS configurations) that would happily accept the open + write calls but discard content.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security.TokenProtection.TokenProtectorFactory.PublishDiagnostics(System.String,System.Exception,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Publishes the diagnostic snapshot for the most recent factory call AND updates the flag to reflect the outcome.
member.remarks: Adversarial-review finding F2 (v1.9.2): the previous implementation only ever SET _degraded to 1 (in DegradeTo) and never cleared it. A subsequent successful CreateFromEnvironment call (transient I/O blip recovered, or a multi-plugin host where one plugin degraded earlier and a later plugin's call would have succeeded) left the flag globally stuck at true — every consumer reading would lie about its own state. Now the flag tracks the *current* call: set to 1 when this call degraded; cleared to 0 when this call succeeded with a real backend.

member.summary: Diagnostic snapshot describing how the active token protector was constructed by . Used for plugin startup logs and regression tests.
member.param: Short label for the active backend (e.g. "dataprotection", "dpapi-user", "null (degraded)").
Resolved key-ring directory when applicable (DataProtection paths only); for backends that don't use a filesystem key store.
Exception that caused a degradation, when the factory fell back from the preferred backend.
Human-readable reason when the factory fell back to . Operators should log this loudly so a misconfigured deployment is visible.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Authentication.OAuthStreamingAuthenticationService`2
member.summary: Base class for OAuth 2.0 + PKCE authentication for streaming services Implements secure OAuth flows with PKCE protection and token management
member.remarks:
Supports streaming services using OAuth 2.0 with PKCE: - Tidal: OAuth 2.0 + PKCE with device flow support - Spotify: OAuth 2.0 + PKCE with various grant types - Apple Music: OAuth-like authentication with developer tokens
Key security features: - PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) prevents authorization code interception - Secure state parameter generation for CSRF protection - Token refresh with automatic retry and fallback - Secure credential storage integration

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Authentication.PKCEGenerator
member.summary: Implementation of PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) generator for OAuth 2.0 Follows RFC 7636 specification for secure authorization code flow
member.remarks:
PKCE prevents authorization code interception attacks by: 1. Client generates a random code_verifier 2. Client sends S256(code_verifier) as code_challenge with auth request 3. Client sends code_verifier when exchanging auth code for tokens 4. Server validates S256(code_verifier) matches original code_challenge
Used by streaming services like Tidal, Spotify, Apple Music for OAuth flows

member.summary: Generates a PKCE code verifier and challenge pair

member.returns: Tuple containing the code verifier and S256 code challenge
member.exception: If length is not between 43 and 128

member.exception: If codeVerifier is null or empty
