member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureDelegatingHandler
member.summary:
that auto-wires an into any built via IHttpClientFactory.AddHttpMessageHandler.
Without this handler, plugins have to wrap every API method (or every adapter) in identical try/catch boilerplate around and . With it, the HTTP layer itself: 1. Calls before sending. 2. Marks the handler bad if the response is 401/403. 3. Marks the handler healthy (success) on any successful 2xx so the gate auto-recovers after the user re-credentials and the next attempt succeeds — no plugin-side code needed for the happy-path reset.
Pair with the existing registration:
services.AddSingleton(sp => new AuthFailureGate(sp.GetRequiredService<IAuthFailureHandler>())); services.AddTransient<AuthFailureDelegatingHandler>(); services.AddHttpClient<IMyApiClient, MyApiClient>() .AddHttpMessageHandler<AuthFailureDelegatingHandler>();

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate
member.summary:
Fail-fast gate built on top of that prevents plugins from hammering an upstream service when authentication is known bad.
Background: a user got IP-banned by Qobuz after the auth/session expired and Lidarr's search loop kept driving the plugin at full rate. Each call returned 401, the plugin propagated the error to Lidarr unchanged, and Lidarr retried. The plugin had no mechanism to say "auth is bad — fail fast, do not even touch the network until the user re-credentials."
Usage on the request side:
gate.EnsureCanProceed(); // throws AuthGatedException if auth latched bad var response = await httpClient.SendAsync(req, ct);
Usage on the response side — prefer the pass-through methods over direct access:
if (response.StatusCode is HttpStatusCode.Unauthorized or HttpStatusCode.Forbidden) { await gate.HandleFailureAsync(new AuthFailure { ErrorCode = "401", Message = "..." }); }
When you genuinely need to probe the upstream once in a while (to detect that the user re-credentialed by some out-of-band path), call first — it returns true at most once per configured probe interval while auth is latched bad.

remarks.para: Cross-ALC constraint: and its are scoped to a single plugin's AssemblyLoadContext. Because Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions is internalised by ILRepack per plugin, the type identity diverges across plugin boundaries — passing a gate (or its handler) from one plugin to another and then calling methods on it will produce InvalidCastException on the internalised types.

In practice each plugin instantiates its own gate via DI and never shares references across plugin boundaries, so this is a constraint to keep in mind rather than an active risk. The pass-through methods (, ) intentionally take the bridged contract types so call sites need not touch directly for the common path.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.Handler
member.summary: The underlying handler. Prefer the gate's own / pass-throughs for everyday wiring — direct handler access is intended for advanced scenarios (custom status read, mocking in tests). Never share this reference across plugin AssemblyLoadContext boundaries; see the class-level cross-ALC remarks.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.HandleFailureAsync(Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.AuthFailure,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Record an authentication failure. Pass-through to to keep call sites independent of 's type identity (see cross-ALC remarks). Equivalent to Handler.HandleFailureAsync(failure, ct).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.HandleSuccessAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Record an authentication success. Pass-through to . Implementations MUST be idempotent (see ).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.ShouldShortCircuit
member.summary:
Consumer-side convenience for bridge entry points (indexer / download-client Search, Download, Test). Returns true when auth is latched bad AND no probe slot is currently available — i.e. the caller should short-circuit (return empty / fail fast) without touching the network. This is the qobuzarr-incident amplification fix: when Lidarr's search loop drives the plugin at full rate, refuse to forward every call upstream until the user re-credentials.
Equivalent to !IsHealthy && !TryAcquireProbeSlot(). NOTE: like , this CONSUMES the per-interval probe slot as a side effect when latched, so call it at most once per entry-point invocation.
Lifted from the byte-identical IsAuthShortCircuited helpers that applemusicarr / tidalarr / qobuzarr each re-implemented at their bridge entry points.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.RecordExceptionOutcome(System.Exception,System.Func{System.Exception,Lidarr.Plugin.Abstractions.Contracts.AuthFailure})
member.summary:
Consumer-side convenience: classify via the supplied delegate and, when it maps to an auth failure, record it on the gate so the gate latches. returns null when the exception is NOT an auth failure (no latch) — this is the only service-specific part each plugin supplies (401/403, plus e.g. apple's "user token" ).
Why this lives in Common: every bridge entry point was duplicating the Task.Run(() => Handler.HandleFailureAsync(failure).AsTask()) .GetAwaiter().GetResult() hop. That sync-over-async pattern (Category A) is a deadlock trap — a direct .GetAwaiter().GetResult() on the handler's can deadlock under Lidarr's single-threaded host because the async continuation is posted back to the blocked context. Encapsulating it once means the error-prone line has a single tested home.
member.param: The exception observed at the bridge entry point.
Maps an exception to an (latch) or null (ignore).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.IsHealthy
member.summary: True when the underlying handler is in a state that allows requests to proceed without restriction (Authenticated or Unknown AND no in-flight failure streak). With failureThreshold > 1, the K-1 failures before latching also flip the gate into rate-limit mode — otherwise the sub-threshold window would re-enable the original hammering scenario.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.Metrics
member.summary: Operator-side observability snapshot. Counters are monotonic across the lifetime of the gate; timestamps are the most recent occurrence of each transition. Read-only.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.EnsureCanProceed
member.summary: Throws when authentication is known bad. Returns silently when auth is healthy or in the indeterminate Unknown state. Reads via the interface so custom handlers carry through the actionable failure detail.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.AcquireProbeSlotWithTimestamp
member.summary: Acquire a probe slot AND return the timestamp it was committed at, so a caller that fails before reaching the upstream (e.g. cancelled HTTP request, DNS failure) can refund the slot via . Returns null when no slot was acquired (gate healthy, or probe rate-limited).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.RefundProbeSlot(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Refund a probe slot previously acquired via , but only if no other caller has grabbed a slot in the meantime (CAS). Use when the network call never executed (cancellation, pre-send error) so the probe budget isn't burned for nothing — the operator's recovery window stays tight.
member.remarks: Known transient anomaly: if caller B is rejected based on the slot held by caller A, then caller A's request fails pre-network and refunds the slot, caller C can immediately acquire — leaving B with an unnecessary rejection while C succeeds. This is a one-shot ordering quirk (B's caller can retry on the next call and will succeed), not a probe-budget violation. Documented rather than guarded because deterministic reproduction requires ms-precise coordination not worth the test infrastructure cost.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.TryAcquireProbeSlot
member.summary: When auth is healthy, always returns true (no rate-limit on probes). When auth is latched bad, returns true at most once per probeInterval, so the plugin can deliberately attempt one network call to detect that the user re-credentialed without spamming the upstream.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGate.ForceReset
member.summary: Forcibly reset the gate to fully open: status back to Unknown, last failure cleared, probe-slot budget cleared. Used by the Reset(key) flow so a settings-UI "Test Connection" can retry immediately after the user supplies fresh credentials. No-ops on non-default handlers.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGateMetrics
member.summary: Operator-side snapshot of an 's lifecycle. All counters are monotonic. Timestamps are the most recent occurrence.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthFailureGateMetrics.#ctor(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Nullable{System.DateTimeOffset},System.Nullable{System.DateTimeOffset})
member.summary: Operator-side snapshot of an 's lifecycle. All counters are monotonic. Timestamps are the most recent occurrence.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.AuthGatedException
member.summary: Thrown by when an outbound request is short-circuited because authentication is latched bad. Carries so callers can surface a delay to the host (instead of every call going to the network and getting 401).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.IAuthFailureGateRegistry
member.summary: Per-key store of instances. Multi-provider plugins (e.g. brainarr — 11 LLM providers) need independent gates per credential: a bad OpenAI key must not gate Anthropic or Ollama.
member.remarks:
Each gate owns its own instance, so per-key status / failure detail / probe budget are fully isolated. Keys are matched case-insensitively (provider id "OpenAI" and "openai" refer to the same gate).
The registry is bounded by maxKeys (default 256) to defend against misconfigured callers passing dynamic keys (e.g. request ids by mistake).

Deprecation (v1.18.0): A Wave-26 adversarial audit found zero non-test plugin consumers across all four ecosystem repos. Every real call-site uses directly (typically via a hand-rolled
keyed per provider, which allows pairing a custom with each gate — something this registry cannot do because it wires internally). This interface and its implementation will be removed in v2.0.0.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.IAuthFailureGateRegistry.Get(System.String)
member.summary: Get or create the gate for a provider key.
member.exception: key is null or whitespace.
key would exceed the configured max.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.IAuthFailureGateRegistry.Reset(System.String)
member.summary: Reset the gate for to its fully-open initial state (status Unknown, no latch, fresh probe budget). The SAME gate instance is preserved — any reference held by a call site stays valid. No-op when the key is not registered.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler
member.summary: with sliding-window failure threshold semantics.

remarks.para: Latches the auth status to after consecutive failures occur within a sliding . Failures older than the window are forgotten — a stale run of 401s against a now-rotated credential won't pre-latch the new one. resets the counter + status to Closed.

This sibling of exists for plugins whose auth-failure recovery patterns are minutes-to-hours (e.g. brainarr's LLM-key rotation cycle) rather than the seconds-to-minutes streaming-session model that the default handler targets. The "open duration" — how often the gate allows a probe while latched — is configured at the GATE layer ( ctor's probeInterval), so this handler only owns the sliding-window threshold.

Pairing this handler with AuthFailureGateRegistry + per-key probeInterval = openDuration produces the brainarr-style "K-of-N-in-W → open for D → probe → close-or-reopen" circuit-breaker behaviour. The same shape as the prior in-brainarr LlmAuthCircuit but built on the shared Common API surface so the ecosystem stays uniform.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler},System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.TimeProvider)
member.param: Required. Diagnostics for latch/recovery transitions and sub-threshold failures.

How many consecutive failures within the window trigger the latch. Default 3 — matches brainarr's LlmAuthCircuit.DefaultConsecutiveFailureThreshold and tolerates transient hiccups (token-refresh races, propagation delays).

Sliding window inside which failures count toward the threshold. Default 5 minutes. A failure older than this is dropped from the counter so a stale 401-run against a rotated credential doesn't pre-latch the new one.
Optional. Pass a fake to make sliding-window tests deterministic.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler.LastFailure
member.summary: Most recent failure details (null after successful auth).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler.ConsecutiveFailureCount
member.summary: Consecutive failures observed within the current sliding window. Exposed for 's sub-threshold rate-limit logic.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler.FirstFailureInWindow
member.summary: First failure in the current sliding window, or null if no failures recorded. Exposed for operator-side observability (how recent is the current run).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Bridge.SlidingWindowAuthFailureHandler.ResetToUnknown
member.summary: Reset the handler back to , clearing any latched failure + counter. Used by the registry's Reset(key) API for settings-UI "Test Connection" flows where the operator has provided fresh credentials and wants to retry without waiting for the probe interval.

member.summary: Compact DTO for caching successful GET responses.

member.summary: Stable, deterministic cache key builder for HTTP GET requests.

member.summary: Builds a stable cache key from request components.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.HotCacheHitMode
member.summary: Controls the hot-cache-hit fast path inside .

remarks.para: Without an opt-in, the executor's only ways to serve a cached body without contacting the origin are , the 304-fold path, and stale-if-error. APIs without ETag or Last-Modified support cannot use 304-fold and must abuse the soft-revalidate window to get "traditional" cache semantics. lets a plugin opt into a plain if (cached && fresh) return cached fast path.
Source: qobuzarr Phase 3b adoption feedback.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.HotCacheHitMode.Disabled
member.summary: Default. The hot-cache-hit fast path is disabled; the executor's existing soft-revalidate, 304-fold, and stale-if-error code paths are the only ways a cached body is returned without contacting the origin.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.HotCacheHitMode.EnabledForFreshEntries
member.summary: Before invoking the resilience pipeline, the executor checks the cache and, if a fresh cached entry is found that is still within its nominal , returns it as a . If validators (ETag / Last-Modified) are present on the cached entry, the cached entry is still returned — no conditional round-trip is attempted.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.HotCacheHitMode.EnabledIgnoringValidators
member.summary: Same as but explicitly ignores any validators on the cached entry. Useful for APIs that emit ETags but where the plugin has decided to treat freshness as authoritative (e.g., the ETag is opaque or the upstream cache headers are unreliable).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy
member.summary: Declarative cache policy describing whether and how long responses should be cached. Immutable and thread-safe.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.VaryByScope
member.summary: When true, caches should vary lookups by a caller-provided scope (e.g., user/tenant).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.SlidingRefreshWindow
member.summary: Optional coalescing window for sliding expiration updates. When set, the cache will extend TTL at most once per this window to avoid stampedes.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.EnableConditionalRevalidation
member.summary: When true, attach conditional validators (ETag/Last-Modified) from cached entries automatically to enable 304 revalidation without external state.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.SoftRevalidateWindow
member.summary: Soft-revalidation budget consumed by . When set, a cached body that is younger than this window is returned without contacting the origin (allowing the caller to revalidate later). Mirrors the APPLEMUSICARR_SOFT_REVALIDATE_DAYS env override that previously existed in plugins.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.StaleIfErrorTtl
member.summary: Stale-if-error budget consumed by . When set, a cached body that is younger than this window is returned (with a Warning header) when the origin returns a 5xx response. Defaults to (disabled). When the origin path supports stale-if-error, plugins typically set this to 7 days (the previous APPLEMUSICARR_STALE_IF_ERROR_DAYS default).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.EvictOnTerminalStatus
member.summary: When , evicts the cached body and any conditional validators on terminal origin statuses (404 or 410), to avoid repeatedly hitting the origin with conditional requests for resources known to be missing. Default .

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.HotHitMode
member.summary: Controls the hot-cache-hit fast path inside . Defaults to (preserves the previous behavior of relying on soft-revalidate, 304-fold, and stale-if-error). See for details.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean,System.TimeSpan,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Extended constructor allowing explicit configuration of .
Full constructor that also controls the soft-revalidate, stale-if-error and terminal-eviction knobs.
Full constructor that also exposes .

Creates a new policy based on this one, optionally overriding properties. Single discoverable entry point that exposes every knob — including the knobs (, , , ) — so IntelliSense surfaces every configurable parameter from one method.
member.remarks: Wave 19: merged the previously-separate WithExecutor(...) entry point into this method to fix a fluent-API discoverability gap. WithExecutor is preserved as an -marked shim that delegates here, so existing callers continue to compile (with a deprecation warning).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy.WithExecutor(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.HotCacheHitMode})
member.summary: Deprecated. The executor knobs (, , , ) have been merged into for fluent-API discoverability. Call policy.With(hotHitMode: ..., ...) directly.
member.remarks: Preserved bit-for-bit so existing call sites continue to compile. Marked (warning, not error) so plugin migrations can be done at the consumer's pace.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.SmartCache`2
member.summary: Intelligent cache with ML-inspired eviction strategies and pattern learning. Provides LFU-LRU hybrid eviction, priority-based TTL, and access pattern tracking.
member.remarks: This implementation is inspired by Qobuzarr's SmartQueryCache and generalized for use across all plugins. Key features: - LFU-LRU hybrid eviction (balances frequency and recency) - Priority-based TTL calculation - Automatic popularity detection - Thread-safe concurrent operations - Comprehensive statistics tracking

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.SmartCache`2.#ctor(System.Func{`0,System.String},Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.SmartCacheOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the SmartCache class.
member.param: Function to convert keys to string for hashing.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.SmartCache`2.#ctor(System.Func{`0,System.String},Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.SmartCacheOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.Func{`1,`1})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the SmartCache class with an optional defensive-copy hook.
member.param: Function to convert keys to string for hashing.

Optional defensive-copy hook applied to every cached value returned from . When (the default), TryGet returns the stored reference directly. For mutable value types (lists, DTOs that callers mutate before redispatching, etc.), pass a function that returns an independent copy so callers cannot mutate the shared cache entry. The hook is not invoked on cache miss. Source: brainarr Phase 3b adoption feedback.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Deduplication.RequestDeduplicator
member.summary: Request deduplication service that prevents cache stampede effects by coalescing concurrent requests Critical for preventing API rate limiting when multiple users simultaneously search for the same content
member.remarks:
Cache Stampede Problem: - 50 users search "Taylor Swift" simultaneously - Without deduplication: 50 parallel API calls for same data - With deduplication: 1 API call, 49 users wait for result
Benefits: - Prevents API rate limiting and potential service bans - Reduces server load and improves response times - Maintains cache consistency across concurrent requests - Provides automatic cleanup of completed requests

member.returns: Result from either the factory function or a concurrent execution

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Diagnostics.HttpExceptionClassifier
member.summary:
Maps exceptions thrown during HTTP calls into a categorical failure + user-readable hint. Plugin Test() methods historically caught generic and surfaced "Test failed: {ex.Message}" which leaked CLR type names ("SocketException", "HttpRequestException") that aren't actionable for end users.
This classifier replaces that pattern with:
catch (Exception ex) { var (category, hint) = HttpExceptionClassifier.Classify(ex); failures.Add(new ValidationFailure("Test", hint)); }
Hints are intentionally short and free of CLR type names; they describe what the user can DO. Specifics (status code, URL) are out of scope — plugins should log those separately for operators.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Globalization.UnicodeNormalizer
member.summary: Unicode normalization service for international artist and album matching Handles complex character encoding issues that cause matching failures for non-English content
member.remarks:
Critical Issue: International artists often fail matching due to: - Different Unicode encodings (UTF-8 vs EUC-KR vs GB2312) - Composed vs decomposed character forms (é vs e+´) - Romanization variations (블랙핑크 vs BLACKPINK vs BlackPink) - Right-to-left text with directional markers - Mixed script content (Latin + Cyrillic + CJK)
This normalizer: 1. Standardizes Unicode forms for consistent comparison 2. Handles romanization and transliteration variants 3. Manages directional text and mixed scripts 4. Provides fuzzy matching for international content 5. Enables optimization for global music catalogs

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.AdaptiveRateLimitingHandler
member.summary:
HttpClient delegating handler that gates every egress request through .
Pre-send: calls to honour token-bucket and active-429-cooldown state. is swallowed — a disposal race must not block the entire pipeline.
Post-send: feeds the response to so the limiter can tighten or relax its adaptive budget.
429 + Retry-After: the header is honoured with a Task.Delay before returning to the caller, so any upstream retry policy (Polly, etc.) waits the right amount of time instead of immediately burning through the remaining budget.
The endpoint key passed to the limiter is derived from the request URI's host and first path segment, giving each logical endpoint its own independent budget while keeping total cardinality bounded.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.AdaptiveRateLimitingHandler.#ctor(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.IUniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Initialises the handler.
member.param: Human-readable service name used as the first key segment in the limiter (e.g. "Tidal", "Qobuz"). Must not be null or whitespace.
Optional logger; when null, 429 warnings are silenced.
Ceiling for honouring a 429 Retry-After. A far-future Retry-After Date (or huge Delta) would otherwise exceed 's ~49.7-day limit and throw. Defaults to 5 minutes when null or negative.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.AdaptiveRateLimitingHandler.BuildEndpointKey(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage)
member.summary:
Derives a stable endpoint key from the request URI. Uses host:firstPathSegment to balance specificity vs. cardinality:
api.tidal.com/v1/search → api.tidal.com:v1
sp-ap-eu.audio.tidal.com/.../seg.mp4 → sp-ap-eu.audio.tidal.com:

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.AdaptiveRateLimitingHandler.ResolveRetryAfter(System.Net.Http.Headers.RetryConditionHeaderValue)
member.summary: Resolves the effective delay from a Retry-After header value. Precedence: Delta → Date → Zero.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.BackendHealthDelegatingHandler
member.summary:
Delegating handler that gates outbound HTTP requests through .
When the target host suffered a connection-class failure (SocketException / DNS) within the last seconds, the call short-circuits immediately with an rather than burning the full retry budget.
On any successful HTTP exchange the down-state is cleared so future calls go through.
The classifyProvider hook controls how the request's host name is mapped to a stable provider key stored in the cache. Two built-in patterns are supported:
Subdomain-mapping (e.g. Tidal): supply a Func<string,string> that maps each host to a logical group. Multiple hosts map to one or more provider keys depending on the topology.
Fixed provider (e.g. Apple Music): use the
convenience overload; all hosts map to the same fixed provider key and the base URL (scheme + host) serves as the fine-grained cache slot.
This gate is independent of AuthFailureGate. Auth-gate trips on repeated 401/403 (auth-failure cascade); this gate trips on repeated connection-refused / DNS failures (network-down cascade). Both can coexist.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.BackendHealthDelegatingHandler.#ctor(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache,System.Func{System.String,System.String},Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Initialises the handler with a custom host→provider mapping function.
member.param: Maps a request host string to a stable provider key. When the host itself is used as the provider key.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.BackendHealthDelegatingHandler.#ctor(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Resilience.BackendHealthCache,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Convenience overload for plugins where all requests map to one fixed provider key (e.g. "apple:music"), while each host's base URL maintains an independent cache slot.
member.param: Provider key applied to every request regardless of host (e.g. "apple:music"). Must not be null or whitespace.

member.summary: Outcome of a invocation, surfaced for telemetry hooks.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.Hit
member.summary: The response was served from a fresh (in-TTL) cache entry without contacting the origin.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.SoftRevalidate
member.summary: The cached entry was within the configured soft-revalidation window; served from cache without contacting the origin (a separate background revalidation may run elsewhere).

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.NotModifiedFold
member.summary: The origin returned 304 Not Modified and the cached body was folded into a synthetic 200 OK.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.StaleIfError
member.summary: The origin returned a 5xx error and a sufficiently fresh cached body was served instead.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.EvictOnTerminal
member.summary: The cache entry (and any conditional validators) was evicted because the origin returned a terminal status (e.g., 404 or 410). The terminal response is returned to the caller as-is.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.Miss
member.summary: The origin was contacted and returned a fresh successful response that was written to the cache.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheHitKind.Passthrough
member.summary: The origin was contacted and returned a non-cacheable response (e.g., non-success that did not trigger stale-if-error or terminal eviction). Returned to the caller without modification.

is the path portion (e.g., "/v1/catalog/us/albums/12345"), and carries any cache-relevant query parameters or scope tokens. Sensitive parameters should already be removed by the caller; the cache implementation also filters known-sensitive keys.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CachingHttpExecutor
member.summary: Single integrated executor for cache-aware, conditional, resilient HTTP GETs across Arr streaming plugins.

remarks.para: This type unifies the ~370-line SendAsync body that previously lived inside applemusicarr's AppleMusicApiClient, qobuzarr's QobuzHttpClient, and brainarr's caching stack. It composes:

item.description: conditional headers (If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since) attached from cache validators or an external ;
soft-window revalidation (return cached body without origin contact while inside );
resilience via (429/Retry-After, exponential backoff, retry budget, per-host concurrency gate);
304 Not Modified fold (synthesize a 200 OK from the cached body, refresh TTL);
2xx caching (buffer once, write to cache + persist validators);
stale-if-error (5xx falls back to a cached body within );
terminal eviction (404/410 evicts validators + cache entry when is true).

remarks.para: The executor is single-tier — it consumes whatever the plugin provides (memory, file, or a SmartCache wrapper). Multi-tier behavior is a cache-implementation concern, not an executor concern.

member.summary: Creates a new executor. is typically an or a -wrapped invoker; derives from .
member.param: HTTP transport that will receive the request after all caching/conditional logic runs.
Resilience policy applied via . When , is used.
Optional policy provider. When supplied, the SendAsync overload that omits a per-call resolves the policy from this provider.
Optional external validator store. Falls back to in-cache validators when null.
Time source — used for soft-revalidate, stale-if-error, and the resilience executor.
Optional logger. Defaults to a no-op logger.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CachingHttpExecutor.SendAsync(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.StreamingApiRequestBuilder,Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CacheKey,Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Caching.CachePolicy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Non-generic convenience overload — equivalent to calling with TPayload = object? and no parse hook. Use this when the caller wants raw bytes only (the most common pattern in applemusicarr/qobuzarr).

member.summary: Builds a request from and sends it through the cache+resilience pipeline. Convenience overload that resolves the from the configured .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CachingHttpHooks`1
member.summary: Optional composition hooks for . All callbacks are best-effort and must not throw; the executor logs and swallows exceptions raised by hook callbacks.
member.typeparam: The parsed payload type produced by the parse hook.
member.param: Parses the buffered response body into a . Called for every code path that produces a payload (fresh origin response, 304 fold, soft-revalidate, stale-if-error). When omitted, is left at its default value.
Last-mile mutation of the outgoing after authentication and conditional headers have been attached. Use to add per-request decorations (e.g., correlation IDs).

Invoked when a cache outcome is decided (after the executor finishes deciding hit/miss/fold/etc.). Useful for incrementing per-endpoint metrics counters.

member.summary: Invoked when a terminal status (e.g., 404, 410) causes the cache entry and conditional validators to be evicted. The is the offending response code.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CachingHttpHooks`1.OnHit
member.summary: Invoked when a cache outcome is decided (after the executor finishes deciding hit/miss/fold/etc.). Useful for incrementing per-endpoint metrics counters.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.CachingHttpHooks`1.PropagateParseExceptions
member.summary: When , exceptions raised by are surfaced to the caller rather than being absorbed into a default payload. Default: (preserves the previous swallow-and-log behavior).
member.remarks: Source: qobuzarr Phase 3b adoption feedback ("legacy callers depend on JsonReaderException propagating from JsonConvert.DeserializeObject"). Plugins adopting strict parsing should set this to true; plugins that prefer the executor's resilience-first behavior should leave it at the default.

member.summary: Shared helpers for extracting well-known HTTP response header values. Accepts the header dictionary as of so the helpers are usable from both -based and NzbDrone.Common.Http.HttpResponse-based call sites without a compile-time dependency on the Lidarr host assemblies.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.HttpResponseHelpers.ParseRetryAfter(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Iterates for a case-insensitive Retry-After entry, tries integer seconds first (clamped to non-negative via ), then an HTTP-date (), and returns the resulting or when the header is absent or unparseable.

member.returns: A non-negative derived from the Retry-After header value, or if the header is absent, empty, or cannot be interpreted as either an integer delta or an HTTP-date.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.PagedResponseValidator
member.summary: Validates that the total number of items collected across all fetched pages of a paged API response matches the server-declared total count.
member.remarks:
Many plugin paged-API consumers iterate pages and accumulate items without ever verifying that the accumulated count equals the totalCount field in the response envelope. This allows silent truncation — where the last page is missing or a server bug returns fewer results — to go undetected.
Call after exhausting all pages and passing the summed item count alongside the totalCount extracted from the first (or last) page envelope. Pass for declaredTotalCount: null when the API does not declare a total (validation is skipped in that case).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.PagedResponseValidator.Validate(System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String)
member.summary: Asserts that equals .
member.param: Sum of page.Items.Count across all fetched pages.
The totalCount (or equivalent) field from the paged response envelope, or if the API does not declare one. When , this method returns without throwing.
Short descriptive name of the operation or resource type, used in the exception message (e.g., "apple-music-albums").
member.exception: Thrown when is not and does not equal .

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.RateLimitHeaderUtilities
member.summary:
Utilities for parsing HTTP rate-limit-related headers, and for deriving stable endpoint keys used by .
Centralises logic that was previously duplicated across plugin-specific DelegatingHandlers (tidalarr's TidalRateLimitingHandler and qobuzarr's QobuzRateLimitingHandler).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.RateLimitHeaderUtilities.ResolveRetryAfter(System.Net.Http.Headers.RetryConditionHeaderValue)
member.summary:
Resolve a (the Retry-After header) to a positive delay, or when the header is absent or describes a time already in the past.
Behaviour mirrors what tidalarr and qobuzarr both implemented inline: - Prefer the delta-seconds form if present. - Otherwise interpret the HTTP-date form as "wait until that absolute UTC time". - Past dates and missing values both return so callers can treat "no wait" uniformly.
member.returns: A non-negative delay; when no wait is implied.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.RateLimitHeaderUtilities.ResolveRetryAfter(System.Net.Http.HttpResponseMessage)
member.summary: Convenience overload that pulls the Retry-After header off a response and resolves it in one step. Returns when the response is null or carries no Retry-After header.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.RateLimitHeaderUtilities.BuildHostFirstSegmentKey(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.String)
member.summary:
Build a rate-limiter endpoint key from a request URI using host + first path segment. This balances specificity (distinct API surfaces get independent budgets) against cardinality (a per-item URL doesn't produce a unique bucket per request).
Examples:
https://api.tidal.com/v1/search?q=foo → api.tidal.com:v1
https://sp-ap-eu.audio.tidal.com/.../seg.mp4 → sp-ap-eu.audio.tidal.com:
https://www.qobuz.com/api.json/0.2/album/get → www.qobuz.com:api.json
member.param: Key to return when the request URI is null.
member.returns: A stable per-endpoint key suitable for keying a rate limiter.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Http.RateLimitHeaderUtilities.BuildHostFirstSegmentKey(System.Uri,System.String)
member.summary: Build a rate-limiter endpoint key from a URI using host + first path segment. See for examples.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Intelligence.CompilationAlbumDetector
member.summary: Specialized detector for compilation albums and various artists scenarios Prevents systematic optimization failures for compilations, soundtracks, and mixed-artist albums UNIVERSAL: All streaming services struggle with Various Artists matching
member.remarks:
Critical Issue: Various Artists albums often fail optimization because: - Lidarr: Album Artist = "Various Artists", individual track artists - Streaming Services: Album Artist = "First Artist" or different attribution pattern - Standard matching fails due to artist mismatch
This detector: 1. Identifies compilation patterns in both platforms 2. Applies specialized matching logic for various artists scenarios 3. Enables optimization for entire category of albums that would otherwise fail 4. Handles soundtracks, tribute albums, genre compilations, DJ mixes

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Intelligence.LiveAlbumNormalizer
member.summary: Live album title normalization service that handles venue information and date variations. Sibling to ; targets cross-streaming-service matching failures for live albums (inconsistent venue formatting, date variations, regional names).
member.remarks:
Cross-plugin issue: Live albums often fail matching due to: - Inconsistent venue formatting: "Live at Madison Square Garden" vs "Live from MSG" - Date format variations: "2023-12-31" vs "December 31, 2023" vs "New Year's Eve 2023" - Regional venue name differences: "Royal Albert Hall" vs "Albert Hall" - Recording context variations: "Live at", "Recorded at", "From", etc.
This normalizer: 1. Identifies live album patterns and extracts core album title 2. Normalizes venue names to canonical forms 3. Standardizes date formats for consistent comparison 4. Creates fuzzy matching patterns for venue variations 5. Enables optimization for live album catalogs that would otherwise fail

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Intelligence.MetadataFieldSanitizer
member.summary: Music-domain text sanitizer for artist, album and version fields coming from streaming-service APIs. Performs control-character / HTML-tag stripping, file-system-safe character substitution and bounded length enforcement.
member.remarks:
This is the cross-plugin core extracted from per-plugin metadata sanitizers. Plugin-specific allow-lists (e.g. dangerous-pattern detection that maps to a service-specific safe default) should remain plugin-local; this class focuses on the universal music-metadata text pipeline.
All regex operations use a 250 ms timeout to mitigate ReDoS risk.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Lyrics.LrclibClient
member.summary:
Minimal client for the LRCLIB public lyrics API (https://lrclib.net/api).
Plugins call as a fallback when their streaming-service's own lyrics endpoint returns nothing. The client intentionally has no caching, no retries beyond the underlying HttpClient, and no rate-limit gating because callers already wire those into their own pipelines.
All failure modes (404, 5xx, network, malformed JSON, empty syncedLyrics) collapse to null. Lyrics are a nice-to-have — a fall-through failure here must never break a download.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Network.NetworkResilienceService
member.summary: Network resilience service for coordinating batch operations and non-HTTP workflows. For outbound HTTP, this service delegates to the unified HTTP executor to prevent drift and avoid double-retry. Use for all HTTP calls; this service remains as a thin adapter for batches and cross-cutting policies.
member.remarks:
Critical Issue: Network failures during batch operations cause: - Partial album downloads with inconsistent state - No recovery mechanism for failed operations - Lost progress on large operations - Corrupted cache states requiring manual cleanup
This service provides: 1. Atomic batch operations with all-or-nothing semantics 2. Automatic retry with exponential backoff for transient failures 3. In-process checkpoint tracking — the batch state survives within a single process lifetime (concurrent batches can find each other by id), but NOT across process restarts. Wiring to durable storage (e.g. JsonFileStore) is a documented future enhancement, not a currently shipped feature. 4. Network health monitoring and circuit breaker pattern 5. Graceful degradation during extended outages

member.summary: Thin adapter that executes a batch of HTTP requests via the shared HTTP resilience executor. Avoids double-retry by delegating all retry behavior to . Batch-level checkpoints/circuit-breaker are still honored.
Executes a batch operation with full resilience including checkpoints and rollback
member.typeparam: Type of input items to process
member.param: Items to process in the batch
member.returns: Results from the batch operation

member.summary: Core batch execution with recovery and checkpoint logic

member.summary: Records a checkpoint timestamp on the in-process batch state so resumed operations can report progress accurately and the periodic save schedule (every _checkpointInterval items) emits a debug line. member.summary: Universal batch memory manager for preventing OOM during large streaming operations Provides adaptive batch sizing, memory monitoring, and streaming processing
member.remarks:
Critical for streaming plugins processing large discographies: - Prevents OutOfMemoryException on 10,000+ album searches - Adapts batch sizes based on available system memory - Provides streaming results for immediate processing - Monitors memory pressure and applies throttling - Supports graceful degradation under memory constraints
Used by Qobuzarr for processing massive classical discographies safely Essential for any plugin dealing with large batch operations

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.NamedServiceRateLimiter
member.summary:
Abstract base for service-specific thin wrappers around . Encapsulates the dispose-guard and delegation boilerplate shared by Tidal, Qobuz, and (optionally) Apple Music plugin-local rate-limiter adapters.
Each plugin subclass typically only overrides what is unique: service-name normalization, stat-helper shortcuts, or (as in Apple's conservative decorator) per-endpoint budget seeding logic.
Lifted as Wave-57 from: Tidalarr/Infrastructure/Performance/TidalRateLimiter.cs, Qobuzarr/Services/Performance/AdaptiveRateLimiter.cs, and AppleMusicarr/Runtime/ConservativeAppleMusicRateLimiter+ConservativeDecorator.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.IUniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter.RecordAuthFailure(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Explicitly signal an authentication failure (401/403 detected via exception or out-of-band). Auth failures are tracked for stats but MUST NOT influence the rate-limit budget — auth is gated by AuthFailureGate, and tightening RPM on credential issues causes the post-recovery throughput to be artificially low.
member.remarks: Default implementation is a no-op so adding this method does not silently break alternate implementations (mocks, custom limiters in downstream plugins). Production limiters SHOULD override to track auth-failure stats.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter
member.summary: Universal adaptive rate limiter supporting multiple streaming services Learns optimal rate limits per service/endpoint and adapts based on response patterns
member.remarks:
Based on Qobuzarr's battle-tested implementation with multi-service support: - Per-service, per-endpoint rate tracking - Success-based rate increases - Failure-based backoff with multiple strategies - Statistical reporting for monitoring - Thread-safe operation across concurrent requests
Streaming service rate limit patterns: - Tidal: ~300-400 req/min, OAuth endpoints more restrictive - Qobuz: ~500-600 req/min, search endpoints can handle higher rates - Spotify: ~100-200 req/min, very strict on certain endpoints

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter.WithConservativeDefaults
member.summary: Creates a rate limiter with the Conservative preset (~1 req/s, 2-60 RPM window).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter.#ctor(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiterOptions)
member.summary: Construct with per-service rate overrides drawn from user-facing settings.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.ServiceRateLimiter.HandleAuthFailureResponse(Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.ServiceRateLimiter.EndpointRateLimit)
member.summary: Track an auth failure without touching the rate-limit budget or the success/error streak counters. Auth gating is the AuthFailureGate's responsibility; the rate limiter would otherwise interfere by tightening the per-endpoint RPM and resetting the streak that drives budget expansion post-recovery.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiterOptions
member.summary:
Fluent options object for .
Lets plugins override the built-in per-service rate caps with a value drawn from user-facing settings (e.g. applemusicarr's "Requests per second" field). Before this type existed, the limiter only had a parameterless ctor with hardcoded defaults — plugin settings that nominally controlled rate were stored but ignored at runtime.
Usage:
var options = new UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiterOptions() .WithServiceLimit("AppleMusic", requestsPerMinute: 60); services.AddSingleton<IUniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter>( _ => new UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiter(options));

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiterOptions.WithServiceLimit(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the starting requests-per-minute rate for the named service. The limiter uses this as the initial cap and lets adaptive backoff vary within bounds derived around it (min = 30% of the value clamped to 1, max = 130% of the value). Returns the same options instance for fluent chaining.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Performance.UniversalAdaptiveRateLimiterOptions.TryGetServiceLimit(System.String,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Look up the user-configured rate for the named service, if any.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Registration.StreamingPluginModule.SuppressHttpClientMetricsFilter(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Removes Microsoft.Extensions.Http.MetricsFactoryHttpMessageHandlerFilter from the service collection. The filter is auto-registered by AddHttpClient via TryAddEnumerable and calls socketsHandler.MeterFactory ??= ... on the primary handler. After ILRepack internalizes M.E.Http into the merged plugin DLL and the plugin loads in an isolated AssemblyLoadContext, the JIT lookup for SocketsHttpHandler.get_MeterFactory throws MissingMethodException — the ALC's System.Net.Http resolution path produces a metadata view in which that property reference can't be bound, despite .NET 8 having the property on the BCL type. We don't surface HttpClient metrics from plugins (Lidarr's host owns that telemetry), so removing the filter is safe and keeps IHttpClientFactory's other functionality (typed-client wiring, connection pooling, delegating-handler chains) intact. Targets only the named filter type, so future Logging / PolicyHttpMessageHandler filters added to M.E.Http are unaffected.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreaker
member.summary:
Advanced circuit breaker with dual-trip logic (consecutive failures OR failure rate), minimum throughput guard, and configurable exception classification.
Designed for WS4.2 parity with Brainarr's circuit breaker semantics.
member.remarks: Key features: - Trips on consecutive failures >= threshold - Trips on failure rate >= threshold when minimum throughput is met - Half-open state requires N successes to close, any failure reopens - TimeProvider injection for deterministic testing - Pluggable exception classification (IsFailure, IsIgnored)

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreaker.ConsecutiveFailures
member.summary: Gets the number of consecutive failures (for testing/diagnostics).

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreaker.FailureRate
member.summary: Gets the current failure rate in the sampling window (0.0 to 1.0).

member.summary: Creates a new AdvancedCircuitBreaker with the specified options and time provider.
member.param: Configuration options. If null, defaults are used.
Time provider for deterministic testing. If null, uses system time.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for AdvancedCircuitBreaker. Supports dual-trip logic (consecutive failures OR failure rate) with minimum throughput guard.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.ConsecutiveFailureThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive failures required to open the circuit. Default: 5

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.FailureRateThreshold
member.summary: Failure rate threshold (0.0 to 1.0) that triggers circuit open. Only applies when MinimumThroughput is met. Default: 0.5 (50%)

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.MinimumThroughput
member.summary: Minimum number of operations in the sampling window before failure rate is considered. Prevents opening on small sample sizes (e.g., 1/2 = 50% but only 2 calls). Default: 10

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.SamplingWindowSize
member.summary: Size of the sliding window for tracking operations. Default: 20

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.BreakDuration
member.summary: Duration the circuit stays open before transitioning to half-open. Default: 30 seconds

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.HalfOpenSuccessThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive successes in half-open state required to close the circuit. Default: 3

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.IsFailure
member.summary: Predicate to determine if an exception should count as a failure. Return true if the exception should trip the breaker. Default: TaskCanceledException, TimeoutException, HttpRequestException count as failures.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.IsIgnored
member.summary: Predicate to determine if an exception should be ignored (not counted at all). Takes precedence over IsFailure - if IsIgnored returns true, IsFailure is not called. Default: null (no exceptions ignored)

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.AdvancedCircuitBreakerOptions.DefaultIsFailure(System.Exception)
member.summary: Default failure classification matching Brainarr behavior. TaskCanceledException, TimeoutException, and HttpRequestException are treated as failures.

member.summary: Creates options with default values matching Brainarr's circuit breaker behavior.

member.summary: Creates aggressive options that trip faster and stay open longer.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.CircularBuffer`1
member.summary: A thread-safe circular buffer (ring buffer) for tracking sliding window data. Used by circuit breakers for failure tracking within a time window.
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the buffer.
member.remarks:
This implementation provides: - O(1) add operations - Thread-safe access via locking - Automatic overwriting of oldest elements when capacity is reached - Efficient memory usage with fixed-size backing array
Common use cases: - Tracking recent failures for circuit breaker decisions - Collecting samples for rate calculation - Maintaining a rolling history of events

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.CircularBuffer`1.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the maximum capacity of the buffer.

member.summary: Gets whether the buffer is full.

member.summary: Creates a new circular buffer with the specified capacity.

member.exception: When capacity is less than 1.

member.summary: Adds an element to the buffer, overwriting the oldest element if full.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker
member.summary: Defines the contract for a circuit breaker implementation. Circuit breakers prevent cascading failures by failing fast when a service is unhealthy.
member.remarks:
The circuit breaker pattern has three states: - Closed: Normal operation, requests pass through - Open: Failure threshold exceeded, requests fail immediately - HalfOpen: Testing if service has recovered
State transitions: - Closed → Open: When failure threshold is exceeded within the window - Open → HalfOpen: After the configured timeout duration - HalfOpen → Closed: When a test request succeeds - HalfOpen → Open: When a test request fails

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.ICircuitBreaker.Name
member.summary: Gets the name/identifier of this circuit breaker instance.

member.summary: Gets the current state of the circuit breaker.

member.summary: Gets whether the circuit breaker allows requests to pass through. True when state is Closed or HalfOpen.

member.summary: Gets statistics about circuit breaker operations.

member.summary: Raised when the circuit breaker state changes.

member.summary: Preset for local LLM providers (Ollama, LM Studio). Short fast retries — when the local service is down a long backoff just delays the user-visible failure; quick retries handle the transient socket-not-ready case.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.ExponentialBackoffRetryPolicy
member.summary: Implementation of exponential backoff retry policy with jitter.
member.remarks:
Exponential Backoff Algorithm with Full Jitter: - Attempt 1: No delay - Attempt 2: Random(0, initialDelay) - Attempt 3: Random(0, initialDelay * 2) - Attempt 4: Random(0, initialDelay * 4) - etc.
Benefits: - Prevents thundering herd problems when multiple instances retry simultaneously - Gives failing services time to recover - Full jitter provides better distribution than decorrelated jitter - Balances quick recovery with system stability
Use cases: - AI provider API calls (network timeouts, rate limits) - Music metadata validation requests - Provider health checks
Non-retryable scenarios: - TaskCanceledException (user cancellation) - Authentication errors (permanent failures - if configured)

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Resilience.TokenBucketRateLimiter
member.summary: Simple token bucket rate limiter implementation.
member.remarks:
Token Bucket Algorithm: - Tokens are added at a fixed rate (refill rate = capacity / period) - Each request consumes one token - If no tokens available, request waits until tokens refill - Maximum tokens capped at capacity
Example: 10 requests per minute - Capacity: 10 tokens - Refill rate: 10/60 = 0.167 tokens per second - Burst: Can handle 10 requests immediately - Sustained: ~0.167 requests per second

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Storage.JsonFileStore`2
member.summary: Generic JSON-backed key/value store with optional TTL and LRU size cap. Persists entries to a single file with atomic replace semantics, and serializes concurrent access through an in-process mutex. Suitable for plugin caches and small mapping stores (UPC -> MBID, ISO -> region, etc.).
member.typeparam: Key type. Must serialize to a JSON-friendly string when is ; for other types, the default serializer rules apply.
Value type. Must be serializable by .

remarks.para: Atomic save: the store writes to a unique temp file then uses (with fallback) to swap the file in. This matches the pattern used by and prevents corruption from crashes during the write.
TTL: when is set, entries older than the TTL are treated as missing on read and quietly purged on the next save.
LRU eviction: when is set, the oldest entries (by last-touch timestamp) are evicted on insert when the store would exceed the cap.

Errors during load (corrupted JSON, IO failures) reset the in-memory state to empty and log a warning. Errors during save are logged but do not throw, matching the behavior of similar stores that prefer "best-effort" persistence.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Storage.JsonFileStore`2.#ctor(System.String,Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Storage.JsonFileStoreOptions{`0},System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Absolute path of the JSON file used for persistence.
Optional store configuration (TTL, LRU cap, key normalization, comparer).

member.summary: Gets the current entry count (best-effort snapshot, includes expired entries until the next save purges them).

member.summary: Stores a value for the specified key. The entry's timestamp is updated to "now", which positions it as the most-recently-used and protects it from immediate eviction. Triggers LRU eviction when is set and the resulting size would exceed the cap.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Services.Validation.DownloadPathValidator
member.summary:
Reusable validator for streaming-plugin DownloadPath settings.
Each plugin used to roll its own check (typically just "did not throw?"), which let traversal segments, relative paths, and other surprises through. This validator returns a structured result with a specific so plugin UIs can show actionable error text instead of generic "invalid path".
Rejection rules:
Empty / whitespace →
Contains .. path segment →
Not absolute →
OS-invalid chars / embedded null / syntactic failure →
This is purely a syntactic/semantic check on the supplied string. It does NOT touch the filesystem — directory existence, writability, and permission probes are out of scope (they belong in a separate connection-test pass so they can be slow + retryable independently).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.Decoders.AnthropicStreamDecoder
member.summary: Decodes Anthropic-format SSE streaming responses (Messages API).

remarks.para: Anthropic streaming wire format:

item.description: Content-Type: text/event-stream
Each event has both an event: name and a JSON data: payload.
Event sequence: message_start, then zero-or-more content_block_* events, then message_delta (carries final usage), then message_stop.
Text deltas arrive in content_block_delta events whose delta.type is text_delta. Reasoning deltas (extended thinking) use thinking_delta and surface as .
There is no [DONE] sentinel; message_stop (or end of stream) terminates.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.Decoders.GeminiStreamDecoder
member.summary: Decodes Google Gemini-format SSE streaming responses.

remarks.para: Gemini streaming format: - Content-Type: text/event-stream - Data: JSON with candidates[].content.parts[].text - Terminator: finishReason in candidates (no [DONE] signal) - Usage: usageMetadata at message end

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.Decoders.OpenAiStreamDecoder
member.summary: Decodes OpenAI-format SSE streaming responses. Compatible with OpenAI API and compatible endpoints (Azure OpenAI, local models, etc.).

remarks.para: OpenAI streaming format: - Content-Type: text/event-stream - Data: JSON with choices[].delta.content - Terminator: data: [DONE]

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.Decoders.ZaiStreamDecoder
member.summary: Decodes Z.AI GLM-format SSE streaming responses.

remarks.para: Z.AI GLM uses an OpenAI-compatible streaming format: - Content-Type: text/event-stream - Data: JSON with choices[].delta.content (OpenAI format) - Terminator: data: [DONE] - Usage: usage object in final chunks
This decoder handles Z.AI-specific extensions like web_search results while delegating standard parsing to OpenAI-compatible logic.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.Decoders.ZaiStreamDecoder.ZaiWebSearch
member.summary: Z.AI-specific web search results (for future use).

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.IStreamDecoder
member.summary: Decodes streaming responses from LLM providers into a unified chunk format. Each provider has its own streaming format (SSE payloads, NDJSON, etc.), and decoders translate these to .

remarks.para: The decoder architecture separates: 1. SSE framing (handled by ) 2. Payload parsing (handled by specific decoders)
For non-SSE streams (like NDJSON), decoders read directly from the stream.

member.summary: Determines if this decoder can handle the given content type.
member.param: The Content-Type header value from the HTTP response.
member.returns: True if this decoder can process the content type.
member.remarks: Multiple decoders may return true for the same content type (e.g., text/event-stream). Use for unambiguous selection.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.IStreamDecoder.CanDecodeForProvider(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Determines if this decoder can handle the given provider and content type. This is the preferred selection method when provider ID is known.
member.param: The provider ID generating the stream.
The Content-Type header value from the HTTP response.
member.returns: True if this decoder can process the stream.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.IStreamDecoder.DecodeAsync(System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Decodes the stream into LLM chunks asynchronously.
member.returns: An async enumerable of decoded chunks.

remarks.para: Implementations should: - Yield chunks as soon as they're available (no buffering) - Handle provider-specific error formats - Set on the final chunk - Include on the last chunk if available
Cancellation should be checked frequently to avoid blocking during cleanup.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.SseFramingReader
member.summary: Reads raw Server-Sent Events (SSE) framing from a stream. This reader handles the SSE wire protocol only (data/event/id/retry fields), without parsing the payload content.

remarks.para: SSE specification: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html
Each event consists of one or more fields followed by a blank line. Fields are: data, event, id, retry. Multiple data fields are concatenated with newlines.

member.name: F:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.SseFramingReader.DefaultMaxEventSize
member.summary: Default maximum event size (1 MB).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.SseFramingReader.#ctor(System.IO.Stream,System.Text.Encoding,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The stream to read SSE data from.
Maximum allowed event size in bytes (default: 1MB). Set to 0 for unlimited.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.SseFramingReader.ReadFramesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads SSE frames from the stream asynchronously.
member.returns: An async enumerable of SSE frames.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.SseFramingReader.SseFrameBuilder
member.summary: Helper class to build SSE frames with multiple data fields.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy
member.summary: Configures timeout behavior for streaming operations. Provides granular control over first-chunk, inter-chunk, and total stream timeouts.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.Default
member.summary: Gets the default timeout policy with conservative defaults.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.FirstChunkTimeout
member.summary: Gets the maximum time to wait for the first chunk of data. If no data arrives within this timeout, the stream fails.
member.remarks: This timeout accounts for: - Network latency to the provider - Provider initialization time - Model loading (for local providers) - Queue wait time (for rate-limited APIs)

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.InterChunkTimeout
member.summary: Gets the maximum time to wait between consecutive chunks. If no data arrives within this timeout after receiving a chunk, the stream is considered stalled.
member.remarks: This timeout detects: - Stalled connections - Provider hangs - Network interruptions mid-stream

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.TotalStreamTimeout
member.summary: Gets the maximum total duration for the entire stream. The stream will be cancelled if this timeout is exceeded, regardless of activity.
member.remarks: This prevents unbounded resource consumption from very long streams. Set to to disable the total timeout (not recommended for production).

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.ForLocalProvider
member.summary: Creates a timeout policy optimized for fast local providers (e.g., Ollama).
member.returns: A policy with shorter timeouts suitable for local inference.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.ForCloudProvider
member.summary: Creates a timeout policy optimized for cloud providers with potential latency.
member.returns: A policy with longer timeouts for cloud-based LLMs.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.ForClaudeCode
member.summary: Creates a timeout policy for the Claude Code CLI provider.
member.returns: A policy tuned for CLI-based streaming.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Streaming.StreamingTimeoutPolicy.Validate
member.summary: Validates that the timeout values are sensible.
member.exception: Thrown if any timeout is negative or zero.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.FileNameSanitizer
member.summary: Utility for sanitizing file and path names to be safe for various file systems.
member.remarks: Prefer Sanitize.PathSegment in Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Security for new code: it uses Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars() directly, performs NFKC Unicode normalization, and rejects unsafe segments via Sanitize.IsSafePath instead of mutating them in-place. FileNameSanitizer is retained for in-flight internal call sites because its replace-with-space behavior for path separators (e.g. "AC/DC" → "AC DC") and "Unknown"-on-empty fallback differ from Sanitize's delete-and-empty semantics.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.HostGateRegistry.Shutdown
member.summary:
Disposes the background sweeper Timer, clears all gate entries, and resets internal state so the registry is safe to use again after the next call to or (the timer is re-created lazily on first access).
Call this during plugin unload / Dispose to prevent the static Timer from continuing to fire against an already-unloaded AssemblyLoadContext. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent) and safe to call even if the registry has never been accessed.

member.summary: Overload that accepts a ResiliencePolicy and maps it to the core implementation. Keeps call sites minimal and avoids argument order mistakes.

Sends a request with resilience and singleflight deduplication for identical GETs. Deduplication shares a buffered HTTP record (status, headers-of-interest, content bytes) across callers, and rebuilds a fresh HttpResponseMessage per consumer to avoid shared disposal issues.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.ResiliencePolicy
member.summary: Describes the resilience characteristics used when executing outbound HTTP or HTTP-like requests. Immutable and thread-safe.

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.ResiliencePolicy.MaxTotalConcurrencyPerHost
member.summary: Aggregate cap across all profiles for a given host. When null, falls back to .

member.name: P:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.ResiliencePolicy.Passthrough
member.summary: "Passthrough" preset for callers whose underlying transport already retries (e.g., Lidarr's NzbDrone.Common.Http.IHttpClient). The executor's treats this as effectively a no-op: MaxRetries=1 (no retries beyond the initial attempt), no per-request timeout, very high concurrency cap, and minimal jitter/backoff. Stacking the executor's resilience layer on top of a transport that already retries leads to multiplied retry counts and explosive backoff — use this preset to keep the resilience pipeline out of the way.
member.remarks: Source: qobuzarr Phase 3b adoption feedback ("the executor's GenericResilienceExecutor must be configured with MaxRetries=1; a ResiliencePolicy.Passthrough preset would document this intent").

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.TestFailureFormatter
member.summary: Formats a consistent user-facing message for plugin Test() catch arms.
member.remarks:
Wave 87 consolidation. Waves 73 (tidalarr), 74 (qobuzarr indexer), and 75 (qobuzarr download client) independently grew the same message shape:
"Test failed (HttpRequestException): connection refused. Full details in Lidarr logs."
This helper centralizes that shape so future plugins (and any new Test() branches) get the same wording for free. The exception type is surfaced because it lets users self-triage at a glance — HttpRequestException signals network, FormatException signals data-shape, AuthenticationException signals credentials. The "Full details in Lidarr logs" pointer reminds operators where to find the stack trace.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Utilities.TestFailureFormatter.Format(System.Exception,System.String)
member.summary: Format an exception caught in a Test() implementation into a single user-facing line that includes the exception type, message, and a pointer to the full log.
member.param: The caught exception. Must not be null.
Optional prefix. Defaults to "Test failed". Pass a more specific phrase (e.g. "Connection test failed") to disambiguate when multiple Test surfaces exist in the same plugin.
member.returns: A formatted string suitable for ValidationFailure messages.

member.name: T:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Validation.TestValidationBuilder
member.summary: Accumulate-then-build helper for plugin Test() pipelines. Collects ALL required-field failures before returning so the user sees every missing field in a single Lidarr "Test" click instead of one failure at a time. This directly fixes the early-return-after-first-missing-field pattern from PR #130 finding #12.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Validation.TestValidationBuilder.RequireNonEmpty(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a if is null, empty, or whitespace. Returns this for fluent chaining. Does NOT return early — every call is always evaluated.
member.param: The settings property name shown in the Lidarr UI error.
Human-readable message. Defaults to " is required." when null.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Validation.TestValidationBuilder.RequireIf(System.Boolean,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Conditional non-empty check: only validates when is true. The plugin idiom this serves is "X is required when Y is/isn't set" — e.g. RequireIf(!Settings.ProbeOnly, "UserToken", Settings.UserToken, "required when ProbeOnly is unchecked"). Cleaner than nesting ifs around chained calls.

member.name: M:Lidarr.Plugin.Common.Validation.TestValidationBuilder.AddFailure(FluentValidation.Results.ValidationFailure)
member.summary: Appends an already-constructed . Useful for failures whose message requires additional context unavailable at chain time.

member.summary: Convenience: append the accumulated failures to an existing list (e.g. the List<ValidationFailure> Lidarr passes to the Test() override). Preserves any failures the host pre-populated, then adds this builder's failures. Equivalent to failures.AddRange(builder.Build()) but spelt as a single fluent-friendly statement.
